addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/slides/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId ã convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName ã use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName ã use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId ã FigJam file, use get_figjam; pass the original board URL as figjamUrl when available
- figma.com/slides/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId ã Figma Slides file

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 ã Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 ã Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints ã but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets ã use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links ã follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations ã follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables ã map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning ã the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the tãÎ [truncated]

attachment.content:
- pr-summary: Summarize changes in a pull request
- api-conventions: API design patterns for this codebase
- explain-code: Explains code with visual diagrams and analogies. Use when explaining how code works, teaching about a codebase, or when the user asks "how does this work?"
- migrate-component: Migrate a component from one framework to another
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 ã 4.6, 4.6 ã 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Identify provenance triple emission in transform scripts

content.text:
The provenance logic is split across two files:

**[emitters.py:82-180](goethe-faust/scripts/transform/emitters.py#L82-L180)** ã `emit_prov_triples()` is where all PROV-O triples are actually constructed. It implements PROV-O Layer 1 (Without-Activity) and emits quads for:
- the CHO as `prov:Entity` with `wasDerivedFrom` (source dataset), `wasAttributedTo` (XSLT agent), and `generatedAtTime`
- the source dataset as `prov:Entity` attributed to the providing institution
- the XSLT stylesheet as `prov:SoftwareAgent` acting on behalf of DDB
- DDB and the provider institution as `prov:Agent` / `foaf:Organization`

**[transform.py:61](goethe-faust/scripts/transform/transform.py#L61)** ã the call site: `streams["prov"] = emit_prov_triples(record, ddb_uri, GRAPH_PROV)`, invoked once per record.

**[constants.py:33](goethe-faust/scripts/transform/constants.py#L33)** ã defines the named graph IRI (`GRAPH_PROV = "https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/prov"`) and the eight PROV-O term URIs (lines 77ã83).

So: all triple generation lives in `emitters.py::emit_prov_triples`, orchestration in `transform.py`, vocabulary constants in `constants.py`.

content.text:
There's provenance handling, but it's called `Prov` / `PROVENANCE_FIELDS` ã not PROV-O. A few things to note:

- **Line 44 / 504**: `PROVENANCE_FIELDS` is a global dict populated during parsing, then stored under `OBJECTS[OBJECT_ID]['Prov']` at the end of each record. This is the raw source metadata (source type, IC codes, LIDO source, provider name/URI/id/isil), not PROV-O triples.
- **Lines 421ã431**: `parse_provenance()` (maps to the `properties` element) and `parse_provider()` (maps to `provider-info`) fill `PROVENANCE_FIELDS` from the XML. The fields collected ã `provider-name`, `provider-uri`, `provider-id`, `provider-ddb-id`, `provider-isil` ã are exactly what `emit_prov_triples()` in your transform scripts reads from `record["provider-info"]`.
- **Line 15**: `ProvenanceStatement` appears in the EDM class list, but that's just part of the EDM vocabulary enumeration, not actively used for PROV-O emission here.

So this script is the **upstream source**: it extracts and bundles provider/source metadata into the JSON record. The actual PROV-O triple emission from that data happens downstream in [emitters.py:82](goethe-faust/scripts/transform/emitters.py#L82).

def emit_prov_triples(record: dict, ddb_cho_uri: str, graph_iri: str) -> NQList:
    """Emit PROV-O Layer 1 (Without-Activity) for one record (ôÏ6.2, ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md ôÏ2)."""
    lines: NQList = []
    props = record.get("properties") or {}
    prov  = record.get("provider-info") or {}

    item_id         = (props.get("item-id")         or "").strip()
    dataset_id      = (props.get("dataset-id")      or "").strip()
    dataset_label   = (props.get("dataset-label")   or "").strip()
    revision        = (props.get("revision-id")     or "").strip()
    ingest_dt       = (props.get("ingest-date")     or "").strip()
    map_ver         = (props.get("mapping-version") or "").strip()
    provider_ddb_id = (prov.get("provider-ddb-id")  or "").strip()
    provider_name   = (prov.get("provider-name")    or "").strip()
    provider_uri    = (prov.get("provider-uri")     or "").strip()
    provider_id     = (prov.get("provider-id")      or "").strip()
    provider_isil   = (prov.get("provider-isil")    or "").strip()

    src_desc  = (record.get("source") or {}).get("description") or {}
    src_ref   = (src_desc.get("record") or {}) if isinstance(src_desc, dict) else {}
    ref_val   = (src_ref.get("ref",  "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""
    src_href  = (src_ref.get("href", "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""
    rec_type  = (src_ref.get("type", "") or "").strip() if isinstance(src_ref, dict) else ""

    ds_uri   = f"urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:{dataset_id}"      if dataset_id      else ""
    xslt_uri = f"urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:{map_ver}"    if map_ver         else ""
    prov_uri = f"urn:ddbedm:provider-info:provider-ddb-id:{provider_ddb_id}" \
               if provider_ddb_id else ""

    # ãã CHO node ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
    cho_nt = f"<{ddb_cho_uri}>"
    lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_ENTITY}>", graph_iri))
    if ds_uri:
        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{PROV_DERIVED}>",   f"<{ds_uri}>",   graph_iri))
    if xslt_uri:
        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{PROV_ATTRIBUTED}>", f"<{xslt_uri}>", graph_iri))
    if ingest_dt:
        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{PROV_GEN_TIME}>",
                             f'"{_escape_literal(ingest_dt)}"^^<{XSD_DATETIME}>', graph_iri))
    if revision:
        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_HAS_VER}>",
                             f'"{_escape_literal(revision)}"', graph_iri))
    if ref_val:
        lines.append(make_nq(cho_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_REF}>",
                             f'"ddb:{_escape_literal(ref_val)}"', graph_iri))

    # ãã Dataset node ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
    if ds_uri:
        ds_nt = f"<{ds_uri}>"
        lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{DCAT_DATASET}>", graph_iri))
        lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_ENTITY}>",  graph_iri))
        lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_ID}>",
                             f'"{_escape_literal(dataset_id)}"', graph_iri))
        if dataset_label:
            lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{RDFS_LABEL}>",
                                 f'"{_escape_literal(dataset_label)}"@de', graph_iri))
        if rec_type:
            lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_TYPE}>", f"<{rec_type}>", graph_iri))
        if prov_uri:
            lines.append(make_nq(ds_nt, f"<{PROV_ATTRIBUTED}>",
                                 f"<{prov_uri}>", graph_iri))

    # ãã XSLT SoftwareAgent node ããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
    if xslt_uri:
        xslt_nt = f"<{xslt_uri}>"
        lines.append(make_nq(xslt_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_SW_AGENT}>", graph_iri))
        lines.append(make_nq(xslt_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_HAS_VER}>",
                             f'"{_escape_literal(map_ver)}"', graph_iri))
        lines.append(make_nq(xslt_nt, f"<{PROV_ON_BEHALF}>", f"<{DDB_BASE}>", graph_iri))

    # ãã DDB Agent node (fixed URI) ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
    ddb_nt = f"<{DDB_BASE}>"
    lines.append(make_nq(ddb_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_AGENT}>", graph_iri))
    lines.append(make_nq(ddb_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{FOAF_ORG}>",   graph_iri))
    lines.append(make_nq(ddb_nt, f"<{FOAF_NAME}>",
                         '"Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek"', graph_iri))

    # ãã Provider Agent node ããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
    if prov_uri:
        prov_nt = f"<{prov_uri}>"
        lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_AGENT}>", graph_iri))
        lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{FOAF_ORG}>",   graph_iri))
        if provider_name:
            lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{FOAF_NAME}>",
                                 f'"{_escape_literal(provider_name)}"', graph_iri))
        if provider_uri:
            lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{SCHEMA_URL}>", f"<{provider_uri}>", graph_iri))
        if provider_id:
            lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{DCTERMS_ID}>",
                                 f'"{_escape_literal(provider_id)}"', graph_iri))
        if provider_isil:
            lines.append(make_nq(prov_nt, f"<{MOCHO_ISIL}>",
                                 f"<{_sanitize_iri(provider_isil)}>", graph_iri))

    # ãã SourceRecord node (one per binary entry under same URI) ããããããããããããããã
    if src_href:
        src_rec_uri = DDB_API_BASE + src_href.lstrip("/")
        src_nt      = f"<{src_rec_uri}>"
        lines.append(make_nq(src_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{PROV_ENTITY}>", graph_iri))
        for binary in coerce_list((record.get("binaries") or {}).get("binary")):
            if not isinstance(binary, dict):
                continue
            ref   = (binary.get("ref")            or "").strip()
            name  = (binary.get("name")           or "").strip()
            name2 = (binary.get("name2")          or "").strip()
            kind  = (binary.get("kind")           or "").strip()

# goethe-faust ã Claude Project Instructions

**Goethe-Faust corpus**: 115,432 DDB records used as the reference implementation
corpus for the mocho ingest pipeline. Scripts and notes here are the primary
development environment for `transform_edm_to_mocho.py` and its dispatch tables.

## Start here

Read `scripts/README.md` for every script: purpose, usage, inputs/outputs,
dependencies. Read `notes/transform-adr.md` for all transform decisions (D1ãD12).
Read `notes/transform-script-plan.md` for the full dc:type dispatch architecture.

Key files:
- `scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py` ã reference transform (JSONL ã N-Triples + JSON-LD)
- `output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` ã dc:type û sector ã rdf:type dispatch table
- `output/config/image_type2class.json` ã mt002 dispatch config
- `notes/transform-adr.md` ã authoritative decisions D1ãD12
- `notes/image-type-class-mapping.md` ã IMAGE dispatch model (D11, D12)
- `data/ddbedm/json_schema_paths.csv` ã JSON schema: all leaf paths + observed value types across the corpus

## Conventions

- No hardcoded absolute paths; no hardcoded secrets
- Scripts are stdlib-only unless a dependency is justified in the header
- All data-derived tables have a backing CSV/JSON before being inlined into a note
- Header block on every script: purpose, usage, inputs/outputs, dependencies, assumptions

## DDB Sector (sparte) and Mediatype reference

**Sector (sparte)**

| Code | Sector |
|---|---|
| sparte001 | Archive |
| sparte002 | Library |
| sparte003 | Monument Preservation |
| sparte004 | Research |
| sparte005 | Media Library |
| sparte006 | Museum |
| sparte007 | Others |

**Mediatype (mt)**

| Code | Mediatype |
|---|---|
| mt001 | Audio |
| mt002 | Photo |
| mt003 | Text |
| mt005 | Video |
| mt007 | Not Digitized |

lastPrompt: there is also a portion in this script which keeps tracks of the software agent. if the software agent triple has already been emitted, there is no need to emit the descriptive triples repeatedly. canãÎ

aiTitle: Identify provenance triple emission in transform scripts

content.text:
No deduplication guard exists in `emitters.py` ã the XSLT SoftwareAgent node triples ([lines 145ã150](goethe-faust/scripts/transform/emitters.py#L145-L150)) and the DDB Agent node triples ([lines 152ã157](goethe-faust/scripts/transform/emitters.py#L152-L157)) are re-emitted for every record. Since `xslt_uri` and `DDB_BASE` are static per run, these identical quads are repeated across all records.

`xml2json-recursive.py` has no such guard either ã it only collects source data, doesn't emit triples.

The optimization you're describing ã emit the descriptive triples for the XSLT agent and DDB agent only once, then just emit the `wasAttributedTo` / `actedOnBehalfOf` link per record ã is currently missing. The typical pattern would be a `_emitted_agents: set` passed into `emit_prov_triples` (or held in a closure/class), checking before appending the descriptive triples. Would you like me to add that?
plan first what to change, also (there needs to be a duckdb lookup to make this happen, right)? finally, update corresponding notes.

content.thinking:
The user wants me to plan what changes to make before implementing. Let me think through this carefully.

The user is asking about deduplication of PROV-O "static" agent triples (XSLT SoftwareAgent and DDB Agent descriptive triples). The question is whether a DuckDB lookup is needed.

Let me think about what "deduplication" means here:

1. **XSLT SoftwareAgent node** (`xslt_uri`): The URI is `urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:{map_ver}`. The `map_ver` comes from `properties.mapping-version` in each record. 

The mapping version isn't truly static since different records could have different versions, though within a single batch it's probably consistent. Multiple records could still share the same `xslt_uri`.

2. **DDB Agent node** (`DDB_BASE`): This is truly static ã always the same URI with the same triples. Definitely only needs to be emitted once.

3. **Provider Agent node** (`prov_uri`): Based on `provider-ddb-id`. Multiple records from the same provider share this URI and could be deduplicated.

4. **Dataset node** (`ds_uri`): Based on `dataset-id`. Multiple records from the same dataset share this URI and could be deduplicated.

Now, about the DuckDB lookup question ã the transform pipeline processes records one at a time from JSONL, so I'd need either an in-memory set to track emitted URIs or a DuckDB lookup for persistence across runs. Given that `sqlite_export.py` already exists in the pipeline, it seems like DuckDB infrastructure is already there. The real question is whether deduplication needs to survive across separate JSONL processing runs or just within a single file.

Looking at the scale ã 65M records in GeMeA and 115,432 in goethe-faust ã and the existing SQLite export pattern, using DuckDB to track which agent URIs have already been written to the output makes sense. That way if a run gets interrupted and resumed, I won't end up with duplicates. Let me examine how `sqlite_export.py` and `transform.py` currently use DuckDB to understand the pattern before implementing this.

1	"""Core transform: orchestrates per-record triple emission."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from collections import Counter
6	
7	from .constants import (
8	    NQList, PropAlign,
9	    MT007_IRI, GRAPH_DDBEDM, GRAPH_MOCHO, GRAPH_PROV, GRAPH_LANG_TITLE,
10	)
11	from .utils import get_object_id, make_nq, mint_bare_id, mint_cho_uri, _extract_mediatype_sector, coerce_list
12	from .emitters import emit_ddbedm_triples, emit_prov_triples, emit_mocho_triples, werk_staging_row
13	
14	_DCTERMS_LANGUAGE = "http://purl.org/dc/terms/language"
15	_ISO639_2_BASE    = "http://id.loc.gov/vocabulary/iso639-2/"
16	
17	
18	def transform_record(
19	    record: dict,
20	    ids_set: set[str] | None,
21	    mediatype_class_map: dict,
22	    htype_map: dict,
23	    audio_type2class: dict,
24	    class_prop_align: PropAlign,
25	    lido_dispatch: dict,
26	) -> tuple[dict[str, NQList], dict | None, dict, dict]:
27	    """Transform one JSONL record into per-graph N-Quads lists (ôÏ7.1).
28	
29	    Returns (streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info).
30	    streams is empty dict when record is filtered by IDs.
31	    dispatch_info: {"target_class", "wemi", "htype_used", "fallback", "is_mt007", "mediatype"}
32	    pred_info: {"ddbedm_classes", "ddbedm_preds", "mocho_preds_all", "mocho_preds_new"} ã Counters
33	    """
34	    obj_id = get_object_id(record)
35	    if obj_id is None:
36	        raise ValueError("Cannot extract object ID from record")
37	
38	    if ids_set is not None and obj_id not in ids_set:
39	        return {}, None, {}, {}
40	
41	    rdf = record["edm"]["RDF"]
42	    cho: dict = rdf.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
43	    if isinstance(cho, list):
44	        cho = cho[0] if cho else {}
45	
46	    about_str = (cho.get("about") or "").strip()
47	    ddb_uri   = mint_bare_id("ProvidedCHO", (about_str.split()[0] if about_str else obj_id))
48	    cho_uri = mint_cho_uri(obj_id)
49	
50	    mediatype, sector = _extract_mediatype_sector(rdf.get("Concept"))
51	    is_mt007 = (mediatype == MT007_IRI)
52	
53	    streams: dict[str, NQList] = {}
54	    lang_coll: set[str] = set()
55	
56	    # Streams [1] and [4] always run, including mt007 (faithfulness + audit trail)
57	    ddbedm_lines, ddbedm_classes, ddbedm_preds, ddbedm_sani = emit_ddbedm_triples(
58	        rdf, GRAPH_DDBEDM, lang_coll,
59	    )
60	    streams["ddbedm"] = ddbedm_lines
61	    streams["prov"]   = emit_prov_triples(record, ddb_uri, GRAPH_PROV)
62	
63	    # Stream [2] mocho and [3] werk: skip mt007 (D15)
64	    werk_row: dict | None = None
65	    dispatch_info: dict = {"target_class": "", "wemi": "", "htype_used": False,
66	                           "fallback": False, "is_mt007": is_mt007, "mediatype": mediatype}
67	    if not is_mt007:
68	        mocho_lines, target_class, wemi, dflags = emit_mocho_triples(
69	            rdf, cho_uri, ddb_uri, sector, mediatype,
70	            mediatype_class_map, htype_map, audio_type2class,
71	            class_prop_align, lido_dispatch, GRAPH_MOCHO,
72	            lang_coll=lang_coll,
73	        )
74	        streams["mocho"] = mocho_lines
75	        werk_row = werk_staging_row(cho_uri, cho, target_class)
76	        mocho_preds_all  = dflags.pop("preds_all",      Counter())

"""Core transform: orchestrates per-record triple emission."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter

from .constants import (
    NQList, PropAlign,
    MT007_IRI, GRAPH_DDBEDM, GRAPH_MOCHO, GRAPH_PROV, GRAPH_LANG_TITLE,
)
from .utils import get_object_id, make_nq, mint_bare_id, mint_cho_uri, _extract_mediatype_sector, coerce_list
from .emitters import emit_ddbedm_triples, emit_prov_triples, emit_mocho_triples, werk_staging_row

_DCTERMS_LANGUAGE = "http://purl.org/dc/terms/language"
_ISO639_2_BASE    = "http://id.loc.gov/vocabulary/iso639-2/"


def transform_record(
    record: dict,
    ids_set: set[str] | None,
    mediatype_class_map: dict,
    htype_map: dict,
    audio_type2class: dict,
    class_prop_align: PropAlign,
    lido_dispatch: dict,
) -> tuple[dict[str, NQList], dict | None, dict, dict]:
    """Transform one JSONL record into per-graph N-Quads lists (ôÏ7.1).

    Returns (streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info).
    streams is empty dict when record is filtered by IDs.
    dispatch_info: {"target_class", "wemi", "htype_used", "fallback", "is_mt007", "mediatype"}
    pred_info: {"ddbedm_classes", "ddbedm_preds", "mocho_preds_all", "mocho_preds_new"} ã Counters
    """
    obj_id = get_object_id(record)
    if obj_id is None:
        raise ValueError("Cannot extract object ID from record")

    if ids_set is not None and obj_id not in ids_set:
        return {}, None, {}, {}

    rdf = record["edm"]["RDF"]
    cho: dict = rdf.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
    if isinstance(cho, list):
        cho = cho[0] if cho else {}

    about_str = (cho.get("about") or "").strip()
    ddb_uri   = mint_bare_id("ProvidedCHO", (about_str.split()[0] if about_str else obj_id))
    cho_uri = mint_cho_uri(obj_id)

    mediatype, sector = _extract_mediatype_sector(rdf.get("Concept"))
    is_mt007 = (mediatype == MT007_IRI)

    streams: dict[str, NQList] = {}
    lang_coll: set[str] = set()

    # Streams [1] and [4] always run, including mt007 (faithfulness + audit trail)
    ddbedm_lines, ddbedm_classes, ddbedm_preds, ddbedm_sani = emit_ddbedm_triples(
        rdf, GRAPH_DDBEDM, lang_coll,
    )
    streams["ddbedm"] = ddbedm_lines
    streams["prov"]   = emit_prov_triples(record, ddb_uri, GRAPH_PROV)

    # Stream [2] mocho and [3] werk: skip mt007 (D15)
    werk_row: dict | None = None
    dispatch_info: dict = {"target_class": "", "wemi": "", "htype_used": False,
                           "fallback": False, "is_mt007": is_mt007, "mediatype": mediatype}
    if not is_mt007:
        mocho_lines, target_class, wemi, dflags = emit_mocho_triples(
            rdf, cho_uri, ddb_uri, sector, mediatype,
            mediatype_class_map, htype_map, audio_type2class,
            class_prop_align, lido_dispatch, GRAPH_MOCHO,
            lang_coll=lang_coll,
        )
        streams["mocho"] = mocho_lines
        werk_row = werk_staging_row(cho_uri, cho, target_class)
        mocho_preds_all  = dflags.pop("preds_all",      Counter())
        mocho_preds_new  = dflags.pop("preds_new",      Counter())
        mocho_uri_sani   = dflags.pop("uri_sanitized",  0)
        mocho_uri_split  = dflags.pop("uri_split",      0)
        dispatch_info.update({"target_class": target_class, "wemi": wemi, **dflags})
    else:
        mocho_preds_all = Counter()
        mocho_preds_new = Counter()
        mocho_uri_sani  = 0
        mocho_uri_split = 0

    if lang_coll:
        ddb_nt = f"<{ddb_uri}>"
        lang_title_lines: NQList = []
        for orig_lang in sorted(lang_coll):
            lang_title_lines.append(make_nq(
                ddb_nt,
                f"<{_DCTERMS_LANGUAGE}>",
                f"<{_ISO639_2_BASE}{orig_lang}>",
                GRAPH_LANG_TITLE,
            ))
        streams[GRAPH_LANG_TITLE] = lang_title_lines

    pred_info: dict = {
        "ddbedm_classes":   ddbedm_classes,
        "ddbedm_preds":     ddbedm_preds,
        "mocho_preds_all":  mocho_preds_all,
        "mocho_preds_new":  mocho_preds_new,
        "uri_sanitized":    ddbedm_sani["uri_sanitized"]  + mocho_uri_sani,
        "uri_split":        ddbedm_sani["uri_split"]      + mocho_uri_split,
        "uri_about_split":  ddbedm_sani["uri_about_split"],
    }
    return streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info

"""Triple emitters: ddbedm passthrough, mocho alignment, PROV-O, and werk_staging."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter

from .constants import (
    AgentDict, NQList, PropAlign,
    RDF_TYPE, RDFS_LABEL, OWL_SAMEAS,
    DCTERMS_SOURCE, DCTERMS_ID, DCTERMS_TYPE, DCTERMS_HAS_VER, DCTERMS_REF,
    DCTERMS_RIGHTS, DCTERMS_CREATOR, DCTERMS_SUBJECT,
    DC_ID, DC_TITLE, DC_DESCRIPTION, DC_SUBJECT, DC_CONTRIBUTOR,
    FOAF_ORG, FOAF_NAME, FOAF_THUMBNAIL,
    SCHEMA_URL, MOCHO_ISIL, MOCHO_NS, MOCHO_AGENT, RICO_HAS_RST,
    PROV_ENTITY, PROV_AGENT, PROV_SW_AGENT,
    PROV_DERIVED, PROV_ATTRIBUTED, PROV_GEN_TIME, PROV_ON_BEHALF,
    DCAT_DATASET, XSD_DATETIME,
    DDB_BASE, DDB_ITEM_BASE, DDB_API_BASE, DDB_HIERARCHY_TYPE, EDM_NS, EDM_HAS_TYPE,
    _HTYPE_PREFIX,
    _EDM_ENTITY_TYPES, _DDBEDM_PROP, _MOCHO_SKIP, _NEW_NS,
    _CLASS_WEMI, _CONTRIBUTOR_COL, _W_SLOT_CLASSES, SUBJECT_KEYS,
)
from .utils import (
    make_nq, coerce_list, mint_bare_id, _escape_literal, _sanitize_iri,
    value_to_nt_obj, normalize_date, is_ddb_or_gnd, resolve_agent, _to_curie,
    build_bare_id_index, expand_obj_nt, resource_uris,
)


def emit_ddbedm_triples(
    rdf: dict,
    graph_iri: str,
    lang_coll: set[str] | None = None,
) -> tuple[NQList, Counter, Counter, Counter]:
    """Emit verbatim EDM passthrough triples for all entity types in rdf (ôÏ6.1).

    Subject: first URI in entity['about']; owl:sameAs emitted for any additional URIs.
    Includes mt007 records.
    Returns (lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr, sani_ctr) where sani_ctr tracks
    uri_sanitized, uri_split, and uri_about_split counts.
    """
    lines:     NQList  = []
    class_ctr: Counter = Counter()
    pred_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
    sani_ctr:  Counter = Counter()
    _skip = frozenset({"about"})
    bare_id_to_uri = build_bare_id_index(rdf)
    for entity_type, entities in rdf.items():
        edm_class = _EDM_ENTITY_TYPES.get(entity_type)
        for entity in coerce_list(entities):
            if not isinstance(entity, dict):
                continue
            raw_about = (entity.get("about") or "").strip()
            if not raw_about:
                continue
            about_parts = raw_about.split()
            subj_uri = mint_bare_id(entity_type, _sanitize_iri(about_parts[0]))
            subj_nt  = f"<{subj_uri}>"
            if len(about_parts) > 1:
                sani_ctr["uri_about_split"] += len(about_parts) - 1
                for alt in about_parts[1:]:
                    lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, f"<{OWL_SAMEAS}>",
                                         f"<{_sanitize_iri(alt)}>", graph_iri))
            if edm_class:
                lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, f"<{RDF_TYPE}>", f"<{edm_class}>", graph_iri))
                class_ctr[_to_curie(edm_class)] += 1
            for key, val in entity.items():
                if key in _skip:
                    continue
                pred_iri = _DDBEDM_PROP.get(key)
                if not pred_iri:
                    continue
                pred_nt = f"<{pred_iri}>"
                curie   = _to_curie(pred_iri)
                for obj_nt in value_to_nt_obj(val, sani_ctr, lang_coll):
                    lines.append(make_nq(subj_nt, pred_nt,
                                         expand_obj_nt(obj_nt, bare_id_to_uri), graph_iri))
                    pred_ctr[curie] += 1
    return lines, class_ctr, pred_ctr, sani_ctr


def emit_prov_triples(record: dict, ddb_cho_uri: str, graph_iri: str) -> NQList:

"""Sequential SQLite ã JSONL export for a single-sector corpus file (Option C)."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import gzip
import json
import logging
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def export(
    db_path: Path,
    out_path: Path,
    *,
    log_interval: int = 100_000,
    limit: int | None = None,
) -> int:
    """Export all records from a single-sector SQLite to a JSONL file.

    Sequential full-table scan of objs ã no per-UID random access.
    Returns total records written.
    """
    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    conn = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path))
    written = errors = 0

    try:
        with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out:
            for uid, buf in conn.execute("SELECT uid, bufgz FROM objs"):
                try:
                    out.write(json.dumps(json.loads(gzip.decompress(buf)), ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
                    written += 1
                except Exception as exc:
                    errors += 1
                    log.warning("%s uid=%s error: %s", db_path.name, uid, exc)

                if written % log_interval == 0:
                    log.info("Exported %d records", written)
                if limit and written >= limit:
                    break
    finally:
        conn.close()

    if errors:
        log.warning("Skipped %d records due to decompress/parse errors", errors)
    log.info("Export complete: %d records ã %s", written, out_path)
    return written


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Export a single-sector s{N}.sqlite to JSONL (Option C prep)"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--db",  type=Path, required=True,
                        help="Path to sector SQLite file (e.g. s2.sqlite)")
    parser.add_argument("--out", type=Path, required=True,
                        help="Output JSONL file path")
    parser.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=None,
                        help="Stop after N records (for dryruns)")
    parser.add_argument("--log-level", default="INFO", dest="log_level",
                        choices=["DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR"])
    args = parser.parse_args()

    logging.basicConfig(
        level=getattr(logging, args.log_level),
        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
    )

    n = export(args.db, args.out, limit=args.limit)
    print(f"{n:,} records ã {args.out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

1	"""
2	Purpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned N-Quads.
3	            Produces four named-graph streams: ddbedm (verbatim EDM passthrough),
4	            mocho (mocho-aligned triples), prov (PROV-O Layer 1), and a DuckDB
5	            werk_staging table for GND Werk linking (link_gnd_works.py, Phase 0).
6	            Reference implementation for the mocho ingest pipeline.
7	            Decisions: transform-adr.md D11/D15/D17, transform-script-adr.md D1ãD27.
8	Usage:      python -m transform
9	                [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--outdir DIR]
10	                [--alignment FILE] [--lido FILE] [--htype FILE]
11	                [--mediatype FILE] [--audio FILE]
12	                [--stats LEVEL] [--log-level LEVEL]
13	                [--limit N] [--total N] [--log-interval N] [--debug]
14	Inputs:     data/items-all-goethe-faust.json              JSONL, one record per line
15	            data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt                  32-char object IDs, one per line
16	            output/config/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv  (target_class, edm_prop) ã target_prop
17	            output/config/lido_event_types.csv             lido_uri ã agent predicates per WEMI
18	            output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv       htype_code ã (rdf_type, rst_iris)
19	            output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv       (sparte, mediatype) ã class dispatch row
20	            output/config/audio_type2class.json            mt001 dc:type ã group (A/B/C)
21	Outputs:    output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/               run directory (one per invocation)
22	              <stem>.nq                                    combined N-Quads (all named graphs)
23	              <stem>-werk-staging.duckdb                  W-slot staging rows
24	              <stem>-stats.json                           run statistics
25	              <stem>-errors.jsonl                         per-record errors (written live)
26	              <stem>.log                                  run log
27	            <stem> is the input filename without extension (e.g. s2 ã s2.nq)
28	Deps:       stdlib only + duckdb (pip install duckdb)
29	Assumes:    JSONL: one JSON object per line; record structure edm.RDF.*
30	            All config CSVs present at output/config/
31	            Graceful exit on SIGINT/SIGTERM: partial stats and errors are written.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import argparse
37	import json
38	import logging
39	import signal
40	import sys
41	import time
42	import traceback
43	from collections import Counter
44	from datetime import datetime
45	from pathlib import Path
46	
47	from .constants import (
48	    DEFAULT_JSONL, DEFAULT_ALIGNMENT, DEFAULT_LIDO, DEFAULT_HTYPE,
49	    DEFAULT_MEDIATYPE, DEFAULT_AUDIO, DEFAULT_OUTPUT_BASE,
50	)
51	from .utils import get_object_id, _to_curie
52	from .loaders import (
53	    load_ids, load_class_prop_alignment, load_lido_event_types,
54	    load_htype_map, load_mediatype_class, load_audio_type2class,
55	)
56	from .transform import transform_record
57	
58	
59	_stop = False
60	
61	
62	def _handle_signal(sig, frame) -> None:
63	    global _stop
64	    _stop = True
65	    print(f"\nSignal {sig} received ã stopping after current record ...", file=sys.stderr)
66	
67	
68	signal.signal(signal.SIGINT,  _handle_signal)
69	signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle_signal)

87	    )

91	                    help="JSONL input file (one DDB-EDM JSON object per line); "
92	                         "default: data/items-all-goethe-faust.json")
93	    io.add_argument("--db",     type=Path, default=None,
94	                    help="SQLite sector file ã reads directly without intermediate JSONL export; "
95	                         "mutually exclusive with --jsonl")
96	    io.add_argument("--offset", type=int,  default=0,
97	                    help="Skip first N rows in SQLite (for parallel workers; use with --db and --limit)")
98	    io.add_argument("--ids",    type=str,  default=None,
99	                    help="Path to ID allowlist file (one 32-char DDB ID per line), "
100	                         "or '-' to read from stdin; omit to process all records")
101	    io.add_argument("--outdir", type=Path, default=None,
102	                    help="Output directory; auto-timestamped if omitted "
103	                         "(default: output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS)")
104	    io.add_argument("--stem", type=str, default=None,

131	    run.add_argument("--stats",     choices=["none", "basic", "dispatch", "full"],
132	                     default="basic",
133	                     help="Stats verbosity written to transform_stats.json: "
134	                          "none=nothing written, basic=run/records/triples/werk_staging, "
135	                          "dispatch=basic+WEMI class counts (recommended for full-corpus runs), "
136	                          "full=dispatch+per-predicate mocho counts (slow ã use with --limit); "
137	                          "default: basic")
138	    run.add_argument("--log-level", default="INFO",
139	                     choices=["DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR"],
140	                     dest="log_level",
141	                     help="Console and file log verbosity; default: INFO")
142	    run.add_argument("--limit",        type=int, default=None,
143	                     help="Stop after N records ã for smoke-testing and sampling")
144	    run.add_argument("--total",        type=int, default=None,
145	                     help="Expected total records ã enables ETA in progress log")
146	    run.add_argument("--log-interval", type=int, default=100_000, dest="log_interval",

148	    run.add_argument("--debug",     action="store_true",
149	                     help="Enable DEBUG logging (shorthand for --log-level DEBUG)")
150	
151	    args = parser.parse_args()
152	    if args.debug:
153	        args.log_level = "DEBUG"
154	
155	    ts     = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
156	    outdir = args.outdir or (DEFAULT_OUTPUT_BASE / ts)
157	    outdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

163	    else:

171	    logging.basicConfig(
172	        filename=str(log_path),
173	        level=getattr(logging, args.log_level),
174	        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
175	    )
176	    log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
177	
178	    log.info("Loading config tables")
179	    class_prop_align    = load_class_prop_alignment(args.alignment)
180	    lido_dispatch       = load_lido_event_types(args.lido)
181	    htype_map           = load_htype_map(args.htype)

191	    try:

200	            ddb_obj_id       VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
201	            cho_uri          VARCHAR,
202	            target_class     VARCHAR,
203	            dc_title         VARCHAR,
204	            dc_alternative   VARCHAR[],
205	            dc_created       VARCHAR,

207	            creator_literals VARCHAR[]
208	        )
209	    """)
210	
211	    stats_level = args.stats
212	
213	    stats:  Counter = Counter()
214	
215	    class_counts: dict[str, Counter] = {"W": Counter(), "E": Counter(), "M": Counter(), "": Counter()}
216	    mt_dist:         Counter = Counter()
217	    ht_dist:         Counter = Counter()
218	    ddbedm_cls:      Counter = Counter()
219	    ddbedm_preds:    Counter = Counter()
220	    mocho_preds_all: Counter = Counter()
221	    mocho_preds_new: Counter = Counter()
222	    werk_by_class:   Counter = Counter()
223	
224	    start_time = time.monotonic()
225	    interrupted = False
226	
227	    def _iter_input():
228	        if args.db:
229	            import gzip
230	            import sqlite3 as _sqlite3
231	            conn = _sqlite3.connect(str(args.db))
232	            try:
233	                q = "SELECT uid, bufgz FROM objs"
234	                if args.offset:
235	                    q += f" LIMIT -1 OFFSET {args.offset}"
236	                for uid, buf in conn.execute(q):

238	                        yield json.dumps(json.loads(gzip.decompress(buf)))
239	                    except Exception as exc:

248	    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
249	         open(errors_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as err_fh:
250	
251	        for line_no, raw in enumerate(_iter_input(), 1):
252	            if _stop:
253	                interrupted = True
254	                log.warning("Interrupted at line %d ã writing partial output", line_no)
255	                break
256	
257	            raw = raw.strip()
258	            if not raw:
259	                continue
260	            if args.limit and line_no > args.limit:
261	                break
262	
263	            try:
264	                record = json.loads(raw)
265	            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
266	                entry = {"line": line_no, "issue": f"JSON parse error: {exc}"}
267	                err_fh.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
268	                stats["json_errors"] += 1
269	                continue
270	
271	            obj_id = get_object_id(record) or f"line:{line_no}"
272	            try:
273	                streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info = transform_record(
274	                    record, ids_set,

276	                    class_prop_align, lido_dispatch,
277	                )
278	            except Exception as exc:
279	                entry = {
280	                    "id":        obj_id,
281	                    "issue":     str(exc),
282	                    "traceback": traceback.format_exc(),
283	                }
284	                err_fh.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
285	                stats["record_errors"] += 1
286	                continue
287	
288	            if not streams:
289	                stats["filtered"] += 1
290	                continue
291	
292	            for graph_name, graph_lines in streams.items():
293	                for nq in graph_lines:
294	                    out.write(nq + "\n")
295	                    stats["triples_total"] += 1
296	                    stats[f"triples_{graph_name}"] += 1
297	
298	            stats["records_processed"]  += 1
299	            stats["uri_sanitized"]      += pred_info.get("uri_sanitized",   0)
300	            stats["uri_split"]          += pred_info.get("uri_split",       0)
301	            stats["uri_about_split"]    += pred_info.get("uri_about_split", 0)
302	
303	            if stats_level in ("dispatch", "full"):
304	                if dispatch_info.get("is_mt007"):
305	                    stats["skipped_mt007"] += 1
306	                elif dispatch_info.get("fallback"):
307	                    stats["dispatch_fallback"] += 1
308	                elif dispatch_info.get("htype_used"):
309	                    stats["dispatch_htype"] += 1
310	                else:
311	                    stats["dispatch_mediatype"] += 1
312	
313	                tc = dispatch_info.get("target_class", "")
314	                if tc:
315	                    wemi = dispatch_info.get("wemi", "M")
316	                    class_counts[wemi][_to_curie(tc)] += 1
317	
318	                mt = (dispatch_info.get("mediatype", "") or "").split("/")[-1]
319	                if mt:
320	                    mt_dist[mt] += 1
321	
322	                rdf_top = record["edm"]["RDF"]
323	                cho_raw = rdf_top.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
324	                if isinstance(cho_raw, list):
325	                    cho_raw = cho_raw[0] if cho_raw else {}
326	                raw_ht = (cho_raw.get("hierarchyType") or "").strip()
327	                if raw_ht:
328	                    ht_dist[raw_ht.replace("htype_", "ht")] += 1
329	
330	                ddbedm_cls.update(pred_info["ddbedm_classes"])
331	                ddbedm_preds.update(pred_info["ddbedm_preds"])
332	                mocho_preds_all.update(pred_info["mocho_preds_all"])
333	                mocho_preds_new.update(pred_info["mocho_preds_new"])
334	
335	            if werk_row and conn is not None:
336	                conn.execute(
337	                    "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO werk_staging VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)",
338	                    [
339	                        werk_row["ddb_obj_id"],
340	                        werk_row["cho_uri"],
341	                        werk_row["target_class"],
342	                        werk_row["dc_title"],
343	                        werk_row["dc_alternative"],
344	                        werk_row["dc_created"],
345	                        werk_row["creator_uris"],
346	                        werk_row["creator_literals"],
347	                    ],
348	                )
349	                stats["werk_staging_rows"] += 1
350	                werk_by_class[_to_curie(werk_row["target_class"])] += 1
351	
352	            processed = stats["records_processed"]
353	            if args.log_interval and processed and processed % args.log_interval == 0:
354	                elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
355	                rate    = processed / elapsed if elapsed > 0 else 0
356	                errors  = stats["json_errors"] + stats["record_errors"]
357	                eta_str = ""
358	                if args.total and rate > 0:
359	                    remaining = (args.total - processed) / rate
360	                    eta_str   = f" | ETA {_fmt_duration(remaining)}"
361	                total_str = f"/{args.total:,}" if args.total else ""
362	                log.info(
363	                    "Progress: %s%s records | triples %d | errors %d"
364	                    " | %.0f rec/s | elapsed %s%s",
365	                    f"{processed:,}", total_str,
366	                    stats["triples_total"], errors,
367	                    rate, _fmt_duration(elapsed), eta_str,
368	                )
369	
370	    if conn is not None:
371	        conn.close()
372	
373	    elapsed_total = time.monotonic() - start_time
374	
375	    if stats_level != "none":
376	        stats_out: dict = {
377	            "run": {
378	                "timestamp":   ts,
379	                "input":       str(args.jsonl),
380	                "stats_level": stats_level,
381	                "elapsed_s":   round(elapsed_total, 1),
382	                "interrupted": interrupted,
383	            },
384	            "records": {
385	                "processed":          stats["records_processed"],
386	                "skipped_not_in_ids": stats["filtered"],
387	                "by_mediatype": dict(mt_dist.most_common()),
388	                "by_htype":     dict(ht_dist.most_common()),
389	                "uri_sanitized":   stats["uri_sanitized"],
390	                "uri_split":       stats["uri_split"],
391	                "uri_about_split": stats["uri_about_split"],
392	                "errors": {
393	                    "json_parse": stats["json_errors"],
394	                    "transform":  stats["record_errors"],
395	                },
396	            },
397	            "triples": {
398	                "total":    stats["triples_total"],
399	                "by_graph": {
400	                    "ddbedm": stats["triples_ddbedm"],
401	                    "mocho":  stats["triples_mocho"],
402	                    "prov":   stats["triples_prov"],
403	                },
404	            },
405	            "werk_staging": {
406	                "rows":     stats["werk_staging_rows"],
407	                "by_class": dict(werk_by_class.most_common()),
408	            },
409	        }
410	
411	        if stats_level in ("dispatch", "full"):
412	            stats_out["dispatch"] = {
413	                "htype_hits":            stats["dispatch_htype"],
414	                "mediatype_hits":        stats["dispatch_mediatype"],
415	                "fallback_d9":           stats["dispatch_fallback"],
416	                "skipped_mt007":         stats["skipped_mt007"],
417	                "work_classes":          dict(class_counts["W"].most_common()),
418	                "expression_classes":    dict(class_counts["E"].most_common()),
419	                "manifestation_classes": dict(class_counts["M"].most_common()),
420	                "rico_classes":          dict(class_counts[""].most_common()),
421	            }
422	            stats_out["ddbedm_classes"] = dict(ddbedm_cls.most_common())
423	            stats_out["ddbedm_vocab"]   = {"properties_all": dict(ddbedm_preds.most_common())}
424	            stats_out["mocho_vocab"]    = {
425	                "properties_all": dict(mocho_preds_all.most_common()),
426	                "properties_new": dict(mocho_preds_new.most_common()),
427	            }
428	
429	        with open(stats_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
430	            json.dump(stats_out, f, indent=2)
431	
432	    status = "Interrupted" if interrupted else "Done"
433	    log.info(
434	        "%s: %d records in %s (%.0f rec/s) | triples %d (mocho %d) | errors %d",
435	        status,
436	        stats["records_processed"],
437	        _fmt_duration(elapsed_total),
438	        stats["records_processed"] / elapsed_total if elapsed_total > 0 else 0,
439	        stats["triples_total"],
440	        stats["triples_mocho"],
441	        stats["record_errors"] + stats["json_errors"],
442	    )
443	
444	
445	if __name__ == "__main__":
446	    main()

"""
Purpose:    Transform DDB-EDM JSONL records to mocho-aligned N-Quads.
            Produces four named-graph streams: ddbedm (verbatim EDM passthrough),
            mocho (mocho-aligned triples), prov (PROV-O Layer 1), and a DuckDB
            werk_staging table for GND Werk linking (link_gnd_works.py, Phase 0).
            Reference implementation for the mocho ingest pipeline.
            Decisions: transform-adr.md D11/D15/D17, transform-script-adr.md D1ãD27.
Usage:      python -m transform
                [--jsonl FILE] [--ids FILE] [--outdir DIR]
                [--alignment FILE] [--lido FILE] [--htype FILE]
                [--mediatype FILE] [--audio FILE]
                [--stats LEVEL] [--log-level LEVEL]
                [--limit N] [--total N] [--log-interval N] [--debug]
Inputs:     data/items-all-goethe-faust.json              JSONL, one record per line
            data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt                  32-char object IDs, one per line
            output/config/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv  (target_class, edm_prop) ã target_prop
            output/config/lido_event_types.csv             lido_uri ã agent predicates per WEMI
            output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv       htype_code ã (rdf_type, rst_iris)
            output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv       (sparte, mediatype) ã class dispatch row
            output/config/audio_type2class.json            mt001 dc:type ã group (A/B/C)
Outputs:    output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/               run directory (one per invocation)
              <stem>.nq                                    combined N-Quads (all named graphs)
              <stem>-werk-staging.duckdb                  W-slot staging rows
              <stem>-stats.json                           run statistics
              <stem>-errors.jsonl                         per-record errors (written live)
              <stem>.log                                  run log
            <stem> is the input filename without extension (e.g. s2 ã s2.nq)
Deps:       stdlib only + duckdb (pip install duckdb)
Assumes:    JSONL: one JSON object per line; record structure edm.RDF.*
            All config CSVs present at output/config/
            Graceful exit on SIGINT/SIGTERM: partial stats and errors are written.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import logging
import signal
import sys
import time
import traceback
from collections import Counter
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

from .constants import (
    DEFAULT_JSONL, DEFAULT_ALIGNMENT, DEFAULT_LIDO, DEFAULT_HTYPE,
    DEFAULT_MEDIATYPE, DEFAULT_AUDIO, DEFAULT_OUTPUT_BASE,
)
from .utils import get_object_id, _to_curie
from .loaders import (
    load_ids, load_class_prop_alignment, load_lido_event_types,
    load_htype_map, load_mediatype_class, load_audio_type2class,
)
from .transform import transform_record


_stop = False


def _handle_signal(sig, frame) -> None:
    global _stop
    _stop = True
    print(f"\nSignal {sig} received ã stopping after current record ...", file=sys.stderr)


signal.signal(signal.SIGINT,  _handle_signal)
signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle_signal)


def _fmt_duration(seconds: float) -> str:
    h, r = divmod(int(seconds), 3600)
    m, s = divmod(r, 60)
    return f"{h:02d}:{m:02d}:{s:02d}"


def main() -> None:
    if len(sys.argv) > 1 and sys.argv[1] == "merge":
        from .merge import main as _merge_main
        sys.argv = [sys.argv[0]] + sys.argv[2:]
        _merge_main()
        return

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Transform DDB-EDM JSONL to mocho N-Quads (ôÏ8)"
    )

    io = parser.add_argument_group("I/O")
    io.add_argument("--jsonl",  type=Path, default=DEFAULT_JSONL,
                    help="JSONL input file (one DDB-EDM JSON object per line); "
                         "default: data/items-all-goethe-faust.json")
    io.add_argument("--db",     type=Path, default=None,
                    help="SQLite sector file ã reads directly without intermediate JSONL export; "
                         "mutually exclusive with --jsonl")
    io.add_argument("--offset", type=int,  default=0,
                    help="Skip first N rows in SQLite (for parallel workers; use with --db and --limit)")
    io.add_argument("--ids",    type=str,  default=None,
                    help="Path to ID allowlist file (one 32-char DDB ID per line), "
                         "or '-' to read from stdin; omit to process all records")
    io.add_argument("--outdir", type=Path, default=None,
                    help="Output directory; auto-timestamped if omitted "
                         "(default: output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS)")
    io.add_argument("--stem", type=str, default=None,
                    help="Output filename stem (overrides the input filename); "
                         "e.g. --stem items-all-goethe-faust ã items-all-goethe-faust.nq, etc.")

    cfg = parser.add_argument_group("Config")
    cfg.add_argument("--alignment", type=Path, default=DEFAULT_ALIGNMENT,
                     help="Property alignment lookup CSV "
                          "(target_class, edm_prop) ã mocho property; "
                          "default: output/config/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv")
    cfg.add_argument("--lido",      type=Path, default=DEFAULT_LIDO,
                     help="LIDO event type dispatch CSV "
                          "(event URI ã agent predicates per WEMI level); "
                          "default: output/config/lido_event_types.csv")
    cfg.add_argument("--htype",     type=Path, default=DEFAULT_HTYPE,
                     help="htype ã class lookup CSV "
                          "(htype_code ã rdf:type IRIs for ôÏ1.1 dispatch); "
                          "default: output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv")
    cfg.add_argument("--mediatype", type=Path, default=DEFAULT_MEDIATYPE,
                     help="Mediatype û sector ã class lookup CSV "
                          "(sector, mediatype ã WEMI class IRIs); "
                          "default: output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv")
    cfg.add_argument("--audio",     type=Path, default=DEFAULT_AUDIO,
                     help="mt001 dc:type ã audio group JSON "
                          "(dc:type value ã A/B/C group for audio class dispatch); "
                          "default: output/config/audio_type2class.json")

    run = parser.add_argument_group("Run control")
    run.add_argument("--stats",     choices=["none", "basic", "dispatch", "full"],
                     default="basic",
                     help="Stats verbosity written to transform_stats.json: "
                          "none=nothing written, basic=run/records/triples/werk_staging, "
                          "dispatch=basic+WEMI class counts (recommended for full-corpus runs), "
                          "full=dispatch+per-predicate mocho counts (slow ã use with --limit); "
                          "default: basic")
    run.add_argument("--log-level", default="INFO",
                     choices=["DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR"],
                     dest="log_level",
                     help="Console and file log verbosity; default: INFO")
    run.add_argument("--limit",        type=int, default=None,
                     help="Stop after N records ã for smoke-testing and sampling")
    run.add_argument("--total",        type=int, default=None,
                     help="Expected total records ã enables ETA in progress log")
    run.add_argument("--log-interval", type=int, default=100_000, dest="log_interval",
                     help="Log a progress line every N records (default: 100000)")
    run.add_argument("--debug",     action="store_true",
                     help="Enable DEBUG logging (shorthand for --log-level DEBUG)")

    args = parser.parse_args()
    if args.debug:
        args.log_level = "DEBUG"

    ts     = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    outdir = args.outdir or (DEFAULT_OUTPUT_BASE / ts)
    outdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    if args.stem:
        stem = args.stem
    elif args.db:
        stem = args.db.stem + (f"-{args.offset}" if args.offset else "")
    else:
        stem = Path(args.jsonl).stem
    out_path    = outdir / f"{stem}.nq"
    werk_path   = outdir / f"{stem}-werk-staging.duckdb"
    stats_path  = outdir / f"{stem}-stats.json"
    errors_path = outdir / f"{stem}-errors.jsonl"
    log_path    = outdir / f"{stem}.log"

    logging.basicConfig(
        filename=str(log_path),
        level=getattr(logging, args.log_level),
        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
    )
    log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

    log.info("Loading config tables")
    class_prop_align    = load_class_prop_alignment(args.alignment)
    lido_dispatch       = load_lido_event_types(args.lido)
    htype_map           = load_htype_map(args.htype)
    mediatype_class_map = load_mediatype_class(args.mediatype)
    audio_type2class    = load_audio_type2class(args.audio)

    ids_set: set[str] | None = None
    if args.ids == "-":
        ids_set = {line.strip() for line in sys.stdin if line.strip()}
    elif args.ids:
        ids_set = load_ids(Path(args.ids))

    try:
        import duckdb
        conn = duckdb.connect(str(werk_path))
    except ImportError:
        log.warning("duckdb not available ã werk_staging will not be written")
        conn = None
    if conn is not None:
        conn.execute("""
        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS werk_staging (
            ddb_obj_id       VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
            cho_uri          VARCHAR,
            target_class     VARCHAR,
            dc_title         VARCHAR,
            dc_alternative   VARCHAR[],
            dc_created       VARCHAR,
            creator_uris     VARCHAR[],
            creator_literals VARCHAR[]
        )
    """)

    stats_level = args.stats

    stats:  Counter = Counter()

    class_counts: dict[str, Counter] = {"W": Counter(), "E": Counter(), "M": Counter(), "": Counter()}
    mt_dist:         Counter = Counter()
    ht_dist:         Counter = Counter()
    ddbedm_cls:      Counter = Counter()
    ddbedm_preds:    Counter = Counter()
    mocho_preds_all: Counter = Counter()
    mocho_preds_new: Counter = Counter()
    werk_by_class:   Counter = Counter()

    start_time = time.monotonic()
    interrupted = False

    def _iter_input():
        if args.db:
            import gzip
            import sqlite3 as _sqlite3
            conn = _sqlite3.connect(str(args.db))
            try:
                q = "SELECT uid, bufgz FROM objs"
                if args.offset:
                    q += f" LIMIT -1 OFFSET {args.offset}"
                for uid, buf in conn.execute(q):
                    try:
                        yield json.dumps(json.loads(gzip.decompress(buf)))
                    except Exception as exc:
                        log.warning("%s uid=%s: %s", args.db.name, uid, exc)
                        yield ""
            finally:
                conn.close()
        else:
            with open(args.jsonl, encoding="utf-8") as f:
                yield from f

    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out, \
         open(errors_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as err_fh:

        for line_no, raw in enumerate(_iter_input(), 1):
            if _stop:
                interrupted = True
                log.warning("Interrupted at line %d ã writing partial output", line_no)
                break

            raw = raw.strip()
            if not raw:
                continue
            if args.limit and line_no > args.limit:
                break

            try:
                record = json.loads(raw)
            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
                entry = {"line": line_no, "issue": f"JSON parse error: {exc}"}
                err_fh.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
                stats["json_errors"] += 1
                continue

            obj_id = get_object_id(record) or f"line:{line_no}"
            try:
                streams, werk_row, dispatch_info, pred_info = transform_record(
                    record, ids_set,
                    mediatype_class_map, htype_map, audio_type2class,
                    class_prop_align, lido_dispatch,
                )
            except Exception as exc:
                entry = {
                    "id":        obj_id,
                    "issue":     str(exc),
                    "traceback": traceback.format_exc(),
                }
                err_fh.write(json.dumps(entry, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
                stats["record_errors"] += 1
                continue

            if not streams:
                stats["filtered"] += 1
                continue

            for graph_name, graph_lines in streams.items():
                for nq in graph_lines:
                    out.write(nq + "\n")
                    stats["triples_total"] += 1
                    stats[f"triples_{graph_name}"] += 1

            stats["records_processed"]  += 1
            stats["uri_sanitized"]      += pred_info.get("uri_sanitized",   0)
            stats["uri_split"]          += pred_info.get("uri_split",       0)
            stats["uri_about_split"]    += pred_info.get("uri_about_split", 0)

            if stats_level in ("dispatch", "full"):
                if dispatch_info.get("is_mt007"):
                    stats["skipped_mt007"] += 1
                elif dispatch_info.get("fallback"):
                    stats["dispatch_fallback"] += 1
                elif dispatch_info.get("htype_used"):
                    stats["dispatch_htype"] += 1
                else:
                    stats["dispatch_mediatype"] += 1

                tc = dispatch_info.get("target_class", "")
                if tc:
                    wemi = dispatch_info.get("wemi", "M")
                    class_counts[wemi][_to_curie(tc)] += 1

                mt = (dispatch_info.get("mediatype", "") or "").split("/")[-1]
                if mt:
                    mt_dist[mt] += 1

                rdf_top = record["edm"]["RDF"]
                cho_raw = rdf_top.get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
                if isinstance(cho_raw, list):
                    cho_raw = cho_raw[0] if cho_raw else {}
                raw_ht = (cho_raw.get("hierarchyType") or "").strip()
                if raw_ht:
                    ht_dist[raw_ht.replace("htype_", "ht")] += 1

                ddbedm_cls.update(pred_info["ddbedm_classes"])
                ddbedm_preds.update(pred_info["ddbedm_preds"])
                mocho_preds_all.update(pred_info["mocho_preds_all"])
                mocho_preds_new.update(pred_info["mocho_preds_new"])

            if werk_row and conn is not None:
                conn.execute(
                    "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO werk_staging VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)",
                    [
                        werk_row["ddb_obj_id"],
                        werk_row["cho_uri"],
                        werk_row["target_class"],
                        werk_row["dc_title"],
                        werk_row["dc_alternative"],
                        werk_row["dc_created"],
                        werk_row["creator_uris"],
                        werk_row["creator_literals"],
                    ],
                )
                stats["werk_staging_rows"] += 1
                werk_by_class[_to_curie(werk_row["target_class"])] += 1

            processed = stats["records_processed"]
            if args.log_interval and processed and processed % args.log_interval == 0:
                elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time
                rate    = processed / elapsed if elapsed > 0 else 0
                errors  = stats["json_errors"] + stats["record_errors"]
                eta_str = ""
                if args.total and rate > 0:
                    remaining = (args.total - processed) / rate
                    eta_str   = f" | ETA {_fmt_duration(remaining)}"
                total_str = f"/{args.total:,}" if args.total else ""
                log.info(
                    "Progress: %s%s records | triples %d | errors %d"
                    " | %.0f rec/s | elapsed %s%s",
                    f"{processed:,}", total_str,
                    stats["triples_total"], errors,
                    rate, _fmt_duration(elapsed), eta_str,
                )

    if conn is not None:
        conn.close()

    elapsed_total = time.monotonic() - start_time

    if stats_level != "none":
        stats_out: dict = {
            "run": {
                "timestamp":   ts,
                "input":       str(args.jsonl),
                "stats_level": stats_level,
                "elapsed_s":   round(elapsed_total, 1),
                "interrupted": interrupted,
            },
            "records": {
                "processed":          stats["records_processed"],
                "skipped_not_in_ids": stats["filtered"],
                "by_mediatype": dict(mt_dist.most_common()),
                "by_htype":     dict(ht_dist.most_common()),
                "uri_sanitized":   stats["uri_sanitized"],
                "uri_split":       stats["uri_split"],
                "uri_about_split": stats["uri_about_split"],
                "errors": {
                    "json_parse": stats["json_errors"],
                    "transform":  stats["record_errors"],
                },
            },
            "triples": {
                "total":    stats["triples_total"],
                "by_graph": {
                    "ddbedm": stats["triples_ddbedm"],
                    "mocho":  stats["triples_mocho"],
                    "prov":   stats["triples_prov"],
                },
            },
            "werk_staging": {
                "rows":     stats["werk_staging_rows"],
                "by_class": dict(werk_by_class.most_common()),
            },
        }

        if stats_level in ("dispatch", "full"):
            stats_out["dispatch"] = {
                "htype_hits":            stats["dispatch_htype"],
                "mediatype_hits":        stats["dispatch_mediatype"],
                "fallback_d9":           stats["dispatch_fallback"],
                "skipped_mt007":         stats["skipped_mt007"],
                "work_classes":          dict(class_counts["W"].most_common()),
                "expression_classes":    dict(class_counts["E"].most_common()),
                "manifestation_classes": dict(class_counts["M"].most_common()),
                "rico_classes":          dict(class_counts[""].most_common()),
            }
            stats_out["ddbedm_classes"] = dict(ddbedm_cls.most_common())
            stats_out["ddbedm_vocab"]   = {"properties_all": dict(ddbedm_preds.most_common())}
            stats_out["mocho_vocab"]    = {
                "properties_all": dict(mocho_preds_all.most_common()),
                "properties_new": dict(mocho_preds_new.most_common()),
            }

        with open(stats_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            json.dump(stats_out, f, indent=2)

    status = "Interrupted" if interrupted else "Done"
    log.info(
        "%s: %d records in %s (%.0f rec/s) | triples %d (mocho %d) | errors %d",
        status,
        stats["records_processed"],
        _fmt_duration(elapsed_total),
        stats["records_processed"] / elapsed_total if elapsed_total > 0 else 0,
        stats["triples_total"],
        stats["triples_mocho"],
        stats["record_errors"] + stats["json_errors"],
    )


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

aiTitle: Identify provenance triple emission in transform scripts

content.thinking:
Now I have a full picture. Let me think through the plan carefully.

**What's happening now:**

In `emit_prov_triples()` ([emitters.py:82-190](goethe-faust/scripts/transform/emitters.py#L82-L190)), for every record, these "static" or "shared" node triples are re-emitted:

1. **XSLT SoftwareAgent node** (lines 144-150): 3 triples ã `rdf:type prov:SoftwareAgent`, `dcterms:hasVersion`, `prov:actedOnBehalfOf DDB_BASE`. The URI is `urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:{map_ver}` ã same across all records with the same mapping version.


2. **DDB Agent node** (lines 152-157): 3 triples defining the Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek as a `prov:Agent` and `foaf:Organization` with a fixed name, using `DDB_BASE` as the URI.
3. **Provider Agent node** (lines 159-174): 4-5 triples describing the provider organization with type, name, URL, identifier, and ISIL code, keyed by a provider-specific URI.
4. **Dataset node** (lines 128-142): 3-5 triples establishing the dataset as both a `dcat:Dataset` and `prov:Entity` with identifier, label, type, and attribution, using a dataset-specific URI.

For deduplication, I don't actually need DuckDBãa simple in-memory set tracking already-emitted URIs during the single-pass stream through the JSONL file is sufficient.

The key nodes to deduplicate are the DDB Agent (always the same 3 triples), the XSLT SoftwareAgent (same triples per map version), and the Provider Agent (same triples per provider), since their descriptive properties don't change across records.

I'll pass an `emitted` set through the call chain from `__main__.py` down to `emit_prov_triples()`, checking it before emitting each shared-node block and adding the URI after the first emission.

For deduplication, a simple in-memory set is all we need since this is a single-pass streaming architecture ã DuckDB would only matter for parallel workers or cross-run persistence.

Now I need to update `emitters.py` to accept the `emitted` parameter and guard the shared-node blocks (DDB agent, XSLT agent, provider, dataset) with deduplication checks.

I'll also update `transform.py` to thread the `emitted_prov` set through to `emit_prov_triples()`, and initialize it in `__main__.py` before the main loop.

Let me check what notes exist in the repository related to PROV-O and the transform architecture.
