content.content:
1	# DDB-EDM PROV-O mapping plan
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-05-01 (updated 2026-05-03)
4	**Status**: In progress
5	**Reference**: `references/rm-018-prov-o.pdf` (slides 11ã16)
6	**ADR**: `notes/transform-adr.md` D11, D12
7	**JSON source**: `data/items-excerpt-1000.json`
8	
9	---
10	
11	## 1. Overview
12	
13	Two provenance layers:
14	
15	| Layer | Granularity | Pattern | Storage |
16	|---|---|---|---|
17	| **1 ã Item** | Per CHO: DDB-EDM source metadata | Without-Activity (slide 13) | Item's own named graph |
18	| **2 ã Graph** | Per named graph: pipeline run that produced it | Full Activity (slide 12) | `<urn:goethe-faust:graph/prov>` |
19	
20	Layer 1 answers: *where does this CHO's data come from in the DDB ecosystem?*
21	Layer 2 answers: *which script run, at what version and time, wrote triples into this graph ã and was inference involved?*
22	
23	---
24	
25	## 2. Layer 1 ã Fine-grained item provenance
26	
27	### 2.1 Graph structure (Without-Activity pattern)
28	
29	Five nodes per item. The full Activity pattern is not used ã added expressivity
30	(blank node or per-item Activity IRI) is not justified for ingest-time metadata.
31	
32	```
33	ddb:CHO ããprov:wasDerivedFromããããããããããã¤ Dataset
34	        ããprov:wasAttributedToããããããããããã¤ XSLT
35	        ããprov:generatedAtTimeããããããããããã¤ "2026-01-07T15:40:43+0100"
36	        ããdcterms:hasVersionããããããããããããã¤ "43"
37	        ããdcterms:referencesããããããããããããã¤ "ddb:<source-ref>"
38	
39	Dataset ããprov:wasAttributedToããããããããããã¤ Provider
40	
41	XSLT    ããprov:actedOnBehalfOfããããããããããã¤ DDB
42	```
43	
44	| Node | PROV-O type | URI pattern |
45	|---|---|---|
46	| CHO | `prov:Entity` | `ddb:item/<properties.item-id>` |
47	| Dataset | `dcat:Dataset`, `prov:Entity` | `urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:<id>` |
48	| XSLT | `prov:SoftwareAgent` | `urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:<ver>` |
49	| Provider | `prov:Agent`, `foaf:Organization` | `urn:ddbedm:provider-info:provider-ddb-id:<id>` |
50	| DDB | `prov:Agent`, `foaf:Organization` | `<http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de>` (fixed) |
51	
52	**URI convention**: `urn:ddbedm:` URNs trace the identifier back to its JSON key
53	chain (`urn:ddbedm:<block>:<key>:<value>`), making the source unambiguous without
54	requiring a dereferenceable endpoint.
55	
56	### 2.2 JSON field ã triple mapping
57	
58	#### 2.2.1 JSON source structure
59	
60	The relevant fields are spread across three top-level blocks:
61	
62	```json
63	{
64	  "properties": {
65	    "item-id":            "222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL",
66	    "dataset-id":         "76409877634279609sQOu",
67	    "dataset-label":      "Gesamtlieferung: Deutsche Fotothek - LIDO",
68	    "revision-id":        "43",
69	    "ingest-date":        "2026-01-07T15:40:43+0100",
70	    "mapping-version":    "6.18",
71	    "cortex-type":        "Kultur"
72	  },
73	  "provider-info": {
74	    "provider-name":      "Deutsche Fotothek",
75	    "provider-uri":       "http://www.deutschefotothek.de",
76	    "provider-id":        "99900890",
77	    "provider-ddb-id":    "CJY7MSLPOPB7FTPC7JM5K2GGM5PBGLYI",
78	    "provider-isil":      "http://ld.zdb-services.de/resource/organisations/DE-2396"
79	  },
80	  "source": {
81	    "description": {
82	      "record": {
83	        "ref":  "222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL",
84	        "href": "/items/222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL/source/record",
85	        "type": "http://www.lido-schema.org/"
86	      }
87	    }
88	  },
89	  "binaries": {
90	    "binary": [
91	      {
92	        "ref":            "0ac6ad6e-a985-4251-91ca-f4b918326ead",
93	        "name":           "Abb. Vorsatz. Titelblatt auf fliegendem Blatt...",
94	        "name2":          "Urheber*in: DDZ (Fotografische Aufnahme)",
95	        "kind":           "http://rightsstatements.org/vocab/InC/1.0/",
96	        "local_pathname": "http://fotothek.slub-dresden.de/fotos/df_pos-2018-a_0000067_000_f.jpg",
97	        "primary":        true
98	      }
99	    ]
100	  }
101	}
102	```
103	
104	#### 2.2.2 CHO (`ddb:item/<properties.item-id>`)
105	
106	| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
107	|---|---|---|
108	| `rdf:type` | ã | `edm:ProvidedCHO`, `prov:Entity` |
109	| `prov:wasDerivedFrom` | `properties.dataset-id` ã Dataset URN | URN |
110	| `prov:wasAttributedTo` | `properties.mapping-version` ã XSLT URN | URN |
111	| `prov:generatedAtTime` | `properties.ingest-date` | xsd:dateTime literal |
112	| `dcterms:hasVersion` | `properties.revision-id` | string literal |
113	| `dcterms:references` | `source.description.record.ref` | `"ddb:<ref>"` literal |
114	
115	#### 2.2.3 Dataset (`urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:<value>`)
116	
117	| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
118	|---|---|---|
119	| `rdf:type` | ã | `dcat:Dataset`, `prov:Entity` |
120	| `dcterms:identifier` | `properties.dataset-id` | string literal |
121	| `rdfs:label` | `properties.dataset-label` | `@de` literal |
122	| `dcterms:type` | `source.description.record.type` | URI |
123	| `prov:wasAttributedTo` | `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` ã Provider URN | URN |
124	
125	#### 2.2.4 XSLT (`urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:<value>`)
126	
127	| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
128	|---|---|---|
129	| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:SoftwareAgent` |
130	| `dcterms:hasVersion` | `properties.mapping-version` | string literal |
131	| `prov:actedOnBehalfOf` | fixed | `<http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de>` |
132	
133	#### 2.2.5 DDB (`<http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de>`)
134	
135	| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
136	|---|---|---|
137	| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:Agent`, `foaf:Organization` |
138	| `foaf:name` | fixed | `"Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek"` |
139	
140	#### 2.2.6 Provider (`urn:ddbedm:provider-info:provider-ddb-id:<value>`)
141	
142	| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
143	|---|---|---|
144	| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:Agent`, `foaf:Organization` |
145	| `foaf:name` | `provider-info.provider-name` | string literal |
146	| `schema:url` | `provider-info.provider-uri` | URI |
147	| `dcterms:identifier` | `provider-info.provider-id` | string literal |
148	| `mocho:isil` | `provider-info.provider-isil` | URI |
149	
150	#### 2.2.7 Source record (`ddb-api:items/<id>/source/record`)
151	
152	URI constructed from `source.description.href` by stripping the leading `/` and
153	prepending `ddb-api: <https://api.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/2/>`.
154	
155	One block of triples per `binaries.binary[i]` entry:
156	
157	| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
158	|---|---|---|
159	| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:Entity` |
160	| `dc:identifier` | `binaries.binary[i].ref` | string literal |
161	| `dc:title` | `binaries.binary[i].name` | `@de` literal |
162	| `dc:description` | concat(`name2 \| name`) | `@de` literal (nulls ã `""`) |
163	| `dcterms:rights` | `binaries.binary[i].kind` | URI |
164	| `dcterms:source` | `binaries.binary[i].local_pathname` | URI |
165	
166	### 2.3 Full Turtle example
167	
168	```turtle
169	@prefix prov:     <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#> .
170	@prefix ddb:      <http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/> .
171	@prefix ddb-api:  <https://api.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/2/> .
172	@prefix dcat:     <http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#> .
173	@prefix dc:       <http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/> .
174	@prefix dcterms:  <http://purl.org/dc/terms/> .
175	@prefix foaf:     <http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/> .
176	@prefix rdfs:     <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
177	@prefix schema:   <https://schema.org/> .
178	@prefix lov:      <http://www.w3.org/ns/iana/media-types/> .
179	
180	# ãã CHO ããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
181	
182	ddb:item/222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL
183	    a prov:Entity ;
184	    prov:wasDerivedFrom
185	        <urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:76409877634279609sQOu> ;
186	    prov:wasAttributedTo
187	        <urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:6.18> ;
188	    prov:generatedAtTime "2026-01-07T15:40:43+0100" ;
189	    dcterms:hasVersion   "43" ;
190	    dcterms:references   "ddb:222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL" .
191	
192	# ãã Dataset ããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
193	
194	<urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:76409877634279609sQOu>
195	    a dcat:Dataset, prov:Entity ;
196	    dcterms:identifier "76409877634279609sQOu" ;
197	    rdfs:label         "Gesamtlieferung: Deutsche Fotothek - LIDO"@de ;
198	    dcterms:type       <http://www.lido-schema.org/> ;
199	    prov:wasAttributedTo
200	        <urn:ddbedm:provider-info:provider-ddb-id:CJY7MSLPOPB7FTPC7JM5K2GGM5PBGLYI> .
201	
202	# ãã XSLT SoftwareAgent ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
203	
204	<urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:6.18>
205	    a prov:SoftwareAgent ;
206	    dcterms:hasVersion "6.18" ;
207	    prov:actedOnBehalfOf <http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de> .
208	
209	# ãã DDB Agent ããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
210	
211	<http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de>
212	    a prov:Agent, foaf:Organization ;
213	    foaf:name "Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek" .
214	
215	# ãã Provider Agent ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
216	
217	<urn:ddbedm:provider-info:provider-ddb-id:CJY7MSLPOPB7FTPC7JM5K2GGM5PBGLYI>
218	    a prov:Agent, foaf:Organization ;
219	    foaf:name          "Deutsche Fotothek" ;
220	    schema:url         <http://www.deutschefotothek.de> ;
221	    dcterms:identifier "99900890" ;
222	    mocho:isil         <http://ld.zdb-services.de/resource/organisations/DE-2396> .
223	
224	# ãã Source record ããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
225	
226	ddb-api:items/222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL/source/record
227	    a prov:Entity ;
228	    dc:identifier  "0ac6ad6e-a985-4251-91ca-f4b918326ead" ;
229	    dc:title       "Abb. Vorsatz. Titelblatt auf fliegendem Blatt..."@de ;
230	    dc:description "Urheber*in: DDZ (Fotografische Aufnahme) | Abb. Vorsatz. Titelblatt..."@de ;
231	    dcterms:rights <http://rightsstatements.org/vocab/InC/1.0/> ;
232	    dcterms:source <http://fotothek.slub-dresden.de/fotos/df_pos-2018-a_0000067_000_f.jpg> .

234	
235	### 2.4 Skipped fields
236	
237	| JSON path | Reason |
238	|---|---|
239	| `properties.cortex-type` | Not modelled in PROV-O graph |
240	| `properties.automatically-translated` | Not modelled in PROV-O graph |
241	| `provider-info.provider-parent-id` | Not modelled in PROV-O graph |
242	| `provider-info.provider-logo` | Not modelled in PROV-O graph |
243	
244	---
245	
246	## 3. Layer 2 ã Graph-level pipeline provenance
247	
248	### 3.1 Design
249	
250	Layer 2 uses the **full Activity pattern** (slide 12): each named graph in the
251	triplestore is a `prov:Entity`; each script run that produced it is a
252	`prov:Activity`; the script binary is a `prov:SoftwareAgent`.
253	
254	All Layer 2 triples are stored in a single meta-graph:
255	`<urn:goethe-faust:graph/prov>`.
256	
257	URI patterns:
258	
259	| Resource | URI pattern |
260	|---|---|
261	| Named graph | `urn:goethe-faust:graph/<name>` |
262	| Script run (Activity) | `urn:goethe-faust:run/<script-stem>/<ISO8601-timestamp>` |
263	| Script agent | `urn:goethe-faust:agent/<script-stem>` |
264	| LLM agent | `urn:goethe-faust:agent/<model-id>` |
265	
266	The `gf:inferenceMethod` property flags the epistemic status of graph contents:
267	
268	| Value | Meaning |
269	|---|---|
270	| `gf:Deterministic` | Pure rule-based transform; output is fully reproducible |
271	| `gf:Heuristic` | Rule-based with uncertain matches (e.g. title-string GND lookup) |
272	| `gf:NER` | Triples derived from NER model output |
273	| `gf:LLMGenerated` | Triples produced or ranked by an LLM |
274	
275	### 3.2 Named graphs catalogue
276	
277	| Named graph | Producing script | Inference method |
278	|---|---|---|
279	| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/transform` | `transform_edm_to_mocho.py` | `gf:Deterministic` |
280	| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/gnd-agents` | `link_gnd_agents.py` | `gf:Heuristic` |
281	| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/gnd-works` | `link_gnd_works.py` | `gf:Heuristic` |
282	| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/ner` | NER script (TBD) | `gf:NER` |
283	| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/llm` | LLM enrichment script (TBD) | `gf:LLMGenerated` |
284	| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/prov` | all scripts (self-referential) | `gf:Deterministic` |
285	
286	### 3.3 Triple mapping
287	
288	Each script run writes the following triples into `<urn:goethe-faust:graph/prov>`.
289	
290	#### 3.3.1 Named graph node (`urn:goethe-faust:graph/<name>`)
291	
292	| Triple | Source | Value type |
293	|---|---|---|
294	| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:Entity` |
295	| `prov:wasGeneratedBy` | runtime | run IRI |
296	| `gf:inferenceMethod` | script config | `gf:` vocab term |
297	
298	#### 3.3.2 Run node (`urn:goethe-faust:run/<script-stem>/<timestamp>`)
299	
300	| Triple | Source | Value type |
301	|---|---|---|
302	| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:Activity` |
303	| `prov:startedAtTime` | runtime | xsd:dateTime |
304	| `prov:endedAtTime` | runtime | xsd:dateTime |
305	| `prov:wasAssociatedWith` | script config | script agent IRI |
306	| `prov:used` | script config | input graph IRI(s) |
307	| `rdfs:comment` | optional | free-text note |
308	
309	#### 3.3.3 Script agent (`urn:goethe-faust:agent/<script-stem>`)
310	
311	| Triple | Source | Value type |
312	|---|---|---|
313	| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:SoftwareAgent` |
314	| `rdfs:label` | script header | filename string |
315	| `dcterms:hasVersion` | script header or git tag | string literal |
316	
317	For LLM agents, add:
318	
319	| Triple | Source | Value type |
320	|---|---|---|
321	| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:SoftwareAgent` |
322	| `rdfs:label` | config | model name string |
323	| `dcterms:hasVersion` | config | model version / API version |
324	| `gf:modelProvider` | config | string literal (e.g. `"Anthropic"`) |
325	
326	### 3.4 Turtle example
327	
328	One run of `transform_edm_to_mocho.py` and one run of `link_gnd_agents.py`,
329	stored in the prov graph.
330	
331	```turtle
332	@prefix prov:    <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#> .
333	@prefix gf:      <urn:goethe-faust:vocab/> .
334	@prefix dcterms: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/> .
335	@prefix rdfs:    <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
336	@prefix xsd:     <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .
337	
338	# ãã Named graph entities ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
339	
340	<urn:goethe-faust:graph/transform>
341	    a prov:Entity ;
342	    prov:wasGeneratedBy <urn:goethe-faust:run/transform_edm_to_mocho/2026-05-03T09:14:22Z> ;
343	    gf:inferenceMethod  gf:Deterministic .
344	
345	<urn:goethe-faust:graph/gnd-agents>
346	    a prov:Entity ;
347	    prov:wasGeneratedBy <urn:goethe-faust:run/link_gnd_agents/2026-05-03T10:02:47Z> ;
348	    gf:inferenceMethod  gf:Heuristic .
349	
350	# ãã Run: transform ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
351	
352	<urn:goethe-faust:run/transform_edm_to_mocho/2026-05-03T09:14:22Z>
353	    a prov:Activity ;
354	    prov:startedAtTime    "2026-05-03T09:14:22Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
355	    prov:endedAtTime      "2026-05-03T09:31:05Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
356	    prov:wasAssociatedWith <urn:goethe-faust:agent/transform_edm_to_mocho> ;
357	    prov:used             <urn:goethe-faust:graph/raw-json> .
358	
359	<urn:goethe-faust:agent/transform_edm_to_mocho>
360	    a prov:SoftwareAgent ;
361	    rdfs:label         "transform_edm_to_mocho.py" ;
362	    dcterms:hasVersion "0.9.1" .
363	
364	# ãã Run: GND agent linking ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
365	
366	<urn:goethe-faust:run/link_gnd_agents/2026-05-03T10:02:47Z>
367	    a prov:Activity ;
368	    prov:startedAtTime    "2026-05-03T10:02:47Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
369	    prov:endedAtTime      "2026-05-03T10:44:19Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
370	    prov:wasAssociatedWith <urn:goethe-faust:agent/link_gnd_agents> ;
371	    prov:used             <urn:goethe-faust:graph/transform> ;
372	    rdfs:comment          "lobid-gnd API; confidence threshold 0.85" .
373	
374	<urn:goethe-faust:agent/link_gnd_agents>
375	    a prov:SoftwareAgent ;
376	    rdfs:label         "link_gnd_agents.py" ;
377	    dcterms:hasVersion "0.3.0" .
378	
379	# ãã Example: LLM enrichment run (when applicable) ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããã
380	
381	<urn:goethe-faust:graph/llm>
382	    a prov:Entity ;
383	    prov:wasGeneratedBy <urn:goethe-faust:run/llm_enrich/2026-05-03T11:00:00Z> ;
384	    gf:inferenceMethod  gf:LLMGenerated .
385	
386	<urn:goethe-faust:run/llm_enrich/2026-05-03T11:00:00Z>
387	    a prov:Activity ;
388	    prov:startedAtTime    "2026-05-03T11:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
389	    prov:endedAtTime      "2026-05-03T11:28:33Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
390	    prov:wasAssociatedWith <urn:goethe-faust:agent/claude-sonnet-4-6> ;
391	    prov:used             <urn:goethe-faust:graph/transform> .
392	
393	<urn:goethe-faust:agent/claude-sonnet-4-6>
394	    a prov:SoftwareAgent ;
395	    rdfs:label         "claude-sonnet-4-6" ;
396	    dcterms:hasVersion "claude-sonnet-4-6" ;
397	    gf:modelProvider   "Anthropic" .
398	```
399	
400	---
401	
402	## 4. Prefix declarations
403	
404	| Prefix | URI |
405	|---|---|
406	| `prov:` | `http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#` |
407	| `gf:` | `urn:goethe-faust:vocab/` |
408	| `ddb:` | `http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/` |
409	| `ddb-api:` | `https://api.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/2/` |
410	| `dcat:` | `http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#` |
411	| `dc:` | `http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/` |
412	| `dcterms:` | `http://purl.org/dc/terms/` |
413	| `foaf:` | `http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/` |
414	| `rdfs:` | `http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#` |
415	| `schema:` | `https://schema.org/` |
416	| `mocho:` | `https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#` |
417	| `xsd:` | `http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#` |

# DDB-EDM PROV-O mapping plan

**Date**: 2026-05-01 (updated 2026-05-03)
**Status**: In progress
**Reference**: `references/rm-018-prov-o.pdf` (slides 11ã16)
**ADR**: `notes/transform-adr.md` D11, D12
**JSON source**: `data/items-excerpt-1000.json`

---

## 1. Overview

Two provenance layers:

| Layer | Granularity | Pattern | Storage |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1 ã Item** | Per CHO: DDB-EDM source metadata | Without-Activity (slide 13) | Item's own named graph |
| **2 ã Graph** | Per named graph: pipeline run that produced it | Full Activity (slide 12) | `<urn:goethe-faust:graph/prov>` |

Layer 1 answers: *where does this CHO's data come from in the DDB ecosystem?*
Layer 2 answers: *which script run, at what version and time, wrote triples into this graph ã and was inference involved?*

---

## 2. Layer 1 ã Fine-grained item provenance

### 2.1 Graph structure (Without-Activity pattern)

Five nodes per item. The full Activity pattern is not used ã added expressivity
(blank node or per-item Activity IRI) is not justified for ingest-time metadata.

```
ddb:CHO ããprov:wasDerivedFromããããããããããã¤ Dataset
        ããprov:wasAttributedToããããããããããã¤ XSLT
        ããprov:generatedAtTimeããããããããããã¤ "2026-01-07T15:40:43+0100"
        ããdcterms:hasVersionããããããããããããã¤ "43"
        ããdcterms:referencesããããããããããããã¤ "ddb:<source-ref>"

Dataset ããprov:wasAttributedToããããããããããã¤ Provider

XSLT    ããprov:actedOnBehalfOfããããããããããã¤ DDB
```

| Node | PROV-O type | URI pattern |
|---|---|---|
| CHO | `prov:Entity` | `ddb:item/<properties.item-id>` |
| Dataset | `dcat:Dataset`, `prov:Entity` | `urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:<id>` |
| XSLT | `prov:SoftwareAgent` | `urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:<ver>` |
| Provider | `prov:Agent`, `foaf:Organization` | `urn:ddbedm:provider-info:provider-ddb-id:<id>` |
| DDB | `prov:Agent`, `foaf:Organization` | `<http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de>` (fixed) |

**URI convention**: `urn:ddbedm:` URNs trace the identifier back to its JSON key
chain (`urn:ddbedm:<block>:<key>:<value>`), making the source unambiguous without
requiring a dereferenceable endpoint.

### 2.2 JSON field ã triple mapping

#### 2.2.1 JSON source structure

The relevant fields are spread across three top-level blocks:

```json
{
  "properties": {
    "item-id":            "222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL",
    "dataset-id":         "76409877634279609sQOu",
    "dataset-label":      "Gesamtlieferung: Deutsche Fotothek - LIDO",
    "revision-id":        "43",
    "ingest-date":        "2026-01-07T15:40:43+0100",
    "mapping-version":    "6.18",
    "cortex-type":        "Kultur"
  },
  "provider-info": {
    "provider-name":      "Deutsche Fotothek",
    "provider-uri":       "http://www.deutschefotothek.de",
    "provider-id":        "99900890",
    "provider-ddb-id":    "CJY7MSLPOPB7FTPC7JM5K2GGM5PBGLYI",
    "provider-isil":      "http://ld.zdb-services.de/resource/organisations/DE-2396"
  },
  "source": {
    "description": {
      "record": {
        "ref":  "222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL",
        "href": "/items/222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL/source/record",
        "type": "http://www.lido-schema.org/"
      }
    }
  },
  "binaries": {
    "binary": [
      {
        "ref":            "0ac6ad6e-a985-4251-91ca-f4b918326ead",
        "name":           "Abb. Vorsatz. Titelblatt auf fliegendem Blatt...",
        "name2":          "Urheber*in: DDZ (Fotografische Aufnahme)",
        "kind":           "http://rightsstatements.org/vocab/InC/1.0/",
        "local_pathname": "http://fotothek.slub-dresden.de/fotos/df_pos-2018-a_0000067_000_f.jpg",
        "primary":        true
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

#### 2.2.2 CHO (`ddb:item/<properties.item-id>`)

| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
|---|---|---|
| `rdf:type` | ã | `edm:ProvidedCHO`, `prov:Entity` |
| `prov:wasDerivedFrom` | `properties.dataset-id` ã Dataset URN | URN |
| `prov:wasAttributedTo` | `properties.mapping-version` ã XSLT URN | URN |
| `prov:generatedAtTime` | `properties.ingest-date` | xsd:dateTime literal |
| `dcterms:hasVersion` | `properties.revision-id` | string literal |
| `dcterms:references` | `source.description.record.ref` | `"ddb:<ref>"` literal |

#### 2.2.3 Dataset (`urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:<value>`)

| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
|---|---|---|
| `rdf:type` | ã | `dcat:Dataset`, `prov:Entity` |
| `dcterms:identifier` | `properties.dataset-id` | string literal |
| `rdfs:label` | `properties.dataset-label` | `@de` literal |
| `dcterms:type` | `source.description.record.type` | URI |
| `prov:wasAttributedTo` | `provider-info.provider-ddb-id` ã Provider URN | URN |

#### 2.2.4 XSLT (`urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:<value>`)

| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
|---|---|---|
| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:SoftwareAgent` |
| `dcterms:hasVersion` | `properties.mapping-version` | string literal |
| `prov:actedOnBehalfOf` | fixed | `<http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de>` |

#### 2.2.5 DDB (`<http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de>`)

| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
|---|---|---|
| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:Agent`, `foaf:Organization` |
| `foaf:name` | fixed | `"Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek"` |

#### 2.2.6 Provider (`urn:ddbedm:provider-info:provider-ddb-id:<value>`)

| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
|---|---|---|
| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:Agent`, `foaf:Organization` |
| `foaf:name` | `provider-info.provider-name` | string literal |
| `schema:url` | `provider-info.provider-uri` | URI |
| `dcterms:identifier` | `provider-info.provider-id` | string literal |
| `mocho:isil` | `provider-info.provider-isil` | URI |

#### 2.2.7 Source record (`ddb-api:items/<id>/source/record`)

URI constructed from `source.description.href` by stripping the leading `/` and
prepending `ddb-api: <https://api.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/2/>`.

One block of triples per `binaries.binary[i]` entry:

| Triple | JSON path | Value type |
|---|---|---|
| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:Entity` |
| `dc:identifier` | `binaries.binary[i].ref` | string literal |
| `dc:title` | `binaries.binary[i].name` | `@de` literal |
| `dc:description` | concat(`name2 \| name`) | `@de` literal (nulls ã `""`) |
| `dcterms:rights` | `binaries.binary[i].kind` | URI |
| `dcterms:source` | `binaries.binary[i].local_pathname` | URI |

### 2.3 Full Turtle example

```turtle

@prefix ddb:      <http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/> .

@prefix rdfs:     <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
@prefix schema:   <https://schema.org/> .
@prefix lov:      <http://www.w3.org/ns/iana/media-types/> .

# ãã CHO ããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã

ddb:item/222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL
    a prov:Entity ;
    prov:wasDerivedFrom
        <urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:76409877634279609sQOu> ;
    prov:wasAttributedTo
        <urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:6.18> ;

# ãã Dataset ããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã

<urn:ddbedm:properties:dataset-id:76409877634279609sQOu>
    a dcat:Dataset, prov:Entity ;
    dcterms:identifier "76409877634279609sQOu" ;
    rdfs:label         "Gesamtlieferung: Deutsche Fotothek - LIDO"@de ;
    dcterms:type       <http://www.lido-schema.org/> ;

        <urn:ddbedm:provider-info:provider-ddb-id:CJY7MSLPOPB7FTPC7JM5K2GGM5PBGLYI> .

# ãã XSLT SoftwareAgent ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã

<urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:6.18>

    dcterms:hasVersion "6.18" ;
    prov:actedOnBehalfOf <http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de> .

# ãã DDB Agent ããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã

# ãã Provider Agent ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã

<urn:ddbedm:provider-info:provider-ddb-id:CJY7MSLPOPB7FTPC7JM5K2GGM5PBGLYI>
    a prov:Agent, foaf:Organization ;

### 2.4 Skipped fields

| JSON path | Reason |
|---|---|
| `properties.cortex-type` | Not modelled in PROV-O graph |
| `properties.automatically-translated` | Not modelled in PROV-O graph |
| `provider-info.provider-parent-id` | Not modelled in PROV-O graph |
| `provider-info.provider-logo` | Not modelled in PROV-O graph |

---

## 3. Layer 2 ã Graph-level pipeline provenance

### 3.1 Design

Layer 2 uses the **full Activity pattern** (slide 12): each named graph in the
triplestore is a `prov:Entity`; each script run that produced it is a
`prov:Activity`; the script binary is a `prov:SoftwareAgent`.

All Layer 2 triples are stored in a single meta-graph:
`<urn:goethe-faust:graph/prov>`.

URI patterns:

| Resource | URI pattern |
|---|---|
| Named graph | `urn:goethe-faust:graph/<name>` |
| Script run (Activity) | `urn:goethe-faust:run/<script-stem>/<ISO8601-timestamp>` |
| Script agent | `urn:goethe-faust:agent/<script-stem>` |
| LLM agent | `urn:goethe-faust:agent/<model-id>` |

The `gf:inferenceMethod` property flags the epistemic status of graph contents:

| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `gf:Deterministic` | Pure rule-based transform; output is fully reproducible |
| `gf:Heuristic` | Rule-based with uncertain matches (e.g. title-string GND lookup) |
| `gf:NER` | Triples derived from NER model output |
| `gf:LLMGenerated` | Triples produced or ranked by an LLM |

### 3.2 Named graphs catalogue

| Named graph | Producing script | Inference method |
|---|---|---|
| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/transform` | `transform_edm_to_mocho.py` | `gf:Deterministic` |
| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/gnd-agents` | `link_gnd_agents.py` | `gf:Heuristic` |
| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/gnd-works` | `link_gnd_works.py` | `gf:Heuristic` |
| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/ner` | NER script (TBD) | `gf:NER` |
| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/llm` | LLM enrichment script (TBD) | `gf:LLMGenerated` |
| `urn:goethe-faust:graph/prov` | all scripts (self-referential) | `gf:Deterministic` |

### 3.3 Triple mapping

Each script run writes the following triples into `<urn:goethe-faust:graph/prov>`.

#### 3.3.1 Named graph node (`urn:goethe-faust:graph/<name>`)

| Triple | Source | Value type |
|---|---|---|
| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:Entity` |
| `prov:wasGeneratedBy` | runtime | run IRI |
| `gf:inferenceMethod` | script config | `gf:` vocab term |

#### 3.3.2 Run node (`urn:goethe-faust:run/<script-stem>/<timestamp>`)

| Triple | Source | Value type |
|---|---|---|
| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:Activity` |
| `prov:startedAtTime` | runtime | xsd:dateTime |
| `prov:endedAtTime` | runtime | xsd:dateTime |
| `prov:wasAssociatedWith` | script config | script agent IRI |
| `prov:used` | script config | input graph IRI(s) |
| `rdfs:comment` | optional | free-text note |

#### 3.3.3 Script agent (`urn:goethe-faust:agent/<script-stem>`)

| Triple | Source | Value type |
|---|---|---|
| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:SoftwareAgent` |
| `rdfs:label` | script header | filename string |
| `dcterms:hasVersion` | script header or git tag | string literal |

For LLM agents, add:

| Triple | Source | Value type |
|---|---|---|
| `rdf:type` | ã | `prov:SoftwareAgent` |
| `rdfs:label` | config | model name string |
| `dcterms:hasVersion` | config | model version / API version |
| `gf:modelProvider` | config | string literal (e.g. `"Anthropic"`) |

### 3.4 Turtle example

One run of `transform_edm_to_mocho.py` and one run of `link_gnd_agents.py`,
stored in the prov graph.

```turtle
@prefix prov:    <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#> .
@prefix gf:      <urn:goethe-faust:vocab/> .
@prefix dcterms: <http://purl.org/dc/terms/> .
@prefix rdfs:    <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
@prefix xsd:     <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .

# ãã Named graph entities ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã

<urn:goethe-faust:graph/transform>
    a prov:Entity ;
    prov:wasGeneratedBy <urn:goethe-faust:run/transform_edm_to_mocho/2026-05-03T09:14:22Z> ;
    gf:inferenceMethod  gf:Deterministic .

<urn:goethe-faust:graph/gnd-agents>
    a prov:Entity ;
    prov:wasGeneratedBy <urn:goethe-faust:run/link_gnd_agents/2026-05-03T10:02:47Z> ;
    gf:inferenceMethod  gf:Heuristic .

# ãã Run: transform ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã

<urn:goethe-faust:run/transform_edm_to_mocho/2026-05-03T09:14:22Z>
    a prov:Activity ;
    prov:startedAtTime    "2026-05-03T09:14:22Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    prov:endedAtTime      "2026-05-03T09:31:05Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    prov:wasAssociatedWith <urn:goethe-faust:agent/transform_edm_to_mocho> ;
    prov:used             <urn:goethe-faust:graph/raw-json> .

<urn:goethe-faust:agent/transform_edm_to_mocho>
    a prov:SoftwareAgent ;
    rdfs:label         "transform_edm_to_mocho.py" ;
    dcterms:hasVersion "0.9.1" .

# ãã Run: GND agent linking ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããããã

<urn:goethe-faust:run/link_gnd_agents/2026-05-03T10:02:47Z>
    a prov:Activity ;
    prov:startedAtTime    "2026-05-03T10:02:47Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    prov:endedAtTime      "2026-05-03T10:44:19Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    prov:wasAssociatedWith <urn:goethe-faust:agent/link_gnd_agents> ;
    prov:used             <urn:goethe-faust:graph/transform> ;
    rdfs:comment          "lobid-gnd API; confidence threshold 0.85" .

<urn:goethe-faust:agent/link_gnd_agents>
    a prov:SoftwareAgent ;
    rdfs:label         "link_gnd_agents.py" ;
    dcterms:hasVersion "0.3.0" .

# ãã Example: LLM enrichment run (when applicable) ãããããããããããããããããããããããããããã

<urn:goethe-faust:graph/llm>
    a prov:Entity ;
    prov:wasGeneratedBy <urn:goethe-faust:run/llm_enrich/2026-05-03T11:00:00Z> ;
    gf:inferenceMethod  gf:LLMGenerated .

<urn:goethe-faust:run/llm_enrich/2026-05-03T11:00:00Z>
    a prov:Activity ;
    prov:startedAtTime    "2026-05-03T11:00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    prov:endedAtTime      "2026-05-03T11:28:33Z"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    prov:wasAssociatedWith <urn:goethe-faust:agent/claude-sonnet-4-6> ;
    prov:used             <urn:goethe-faust:graph/transform> .

<urn:goethe-faust:agent/claude-sonnet-4-6>
    a prov:SoftwareAgent ;
    rdfs:label         "claude-sonnet-4-6" ;
    dcterms:hasVersion "claude-sonnet-4-6" ;
    gf:modelProvider   "Anthropic" .
```

---

## 4. Prefix declarations

| Prefix | URI |
|---|---|
| `prov:` | `http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#` |
| `gf:` | `urn:goethe-faust:vocab/` |
| `ddb:` | `http://www.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/` |
| `ddb-api:` | `https://api.deutsche-digitale-bibliothek.de/2/` |
| `dcat:` | `http://www.w3.org/ns/dcat#` |
| `dc:` | `http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/` |
| `dcterms:` | `http://purl.org/dc/terms/` |
| `foaf:` | `http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/` |
| `rdfs:` | `http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#` |
| `schema:` | `https://schema.org/` |
| `mocho:` | `https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#` |
| `xsd:` | `http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#` |

content.content:
1	# ADR: transform_edm_to_mocho.py ã Implementation Decisions
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-04-14  
4	**Status**: Accepted  
5	**Related**: `transform-adr.md` (D0 ã design), `transform-props-mapping-adr.md` (property decisions ã D6/D7/D8 moved there), `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md`
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Context
10	
11	This document records implementation decisions for `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`,
12	the reference DDB-EDM JSONL ã mocho RDF transform. Dispatch architecture and
13	signal priority are decided in `transform-adr.md` (D0); the decisions here
14	govern how those design choices are realised in code.
15	
16	---
17	
18	## Decision 1: Use JSONL as input, not the NT file
19	
20	**Decision**: Stream `items-all-goethe-faust.json` (JSONL, one JSON object per
21	line). The NT file `ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt` is not used.
22	
23	**Alternatives considered**:
24	- *NT file*: Load triples from the pre-generated NT and align by predicate IRI.
25	  Rejected because: (a) the NT was generated from the same JSONL and carries no
26	  additional information; (b) the alignment table is keyed by `(entity_type,
27	  json_key)` matching the JSON structure, not by IRI, so NT input would require
28	  a reverse IRIãjson_key lookup that is neither defined nor stable.
29	
30	**Rationale**: JSONL streams with constant memory, preserves the `(entity_type,
31	json_key)` lookup key structure, and requires only stdlib `json`. No NT indexing
32	phase needed.
33	
34	---
35	
36	## Decision 2: stdlib only ã no rdflib in the transform script
37	
38	**Decision**: The transform script uses only Python stdlib (`json`, `csv`, `re`,
39	`collections`, `argparse`, `pathlib`). rdflib is not imported.
40	
41	**Rationale**: The transform is a data pipeline step, not an ontology processing
42	step. N-Triples output can be constructed by string formatting. rdflib adds a
43	non-stdlib dependency and significant startup overhead for a script that runs
44	over 115k records.
45	
46	**Scope**: This applies only to `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`. Upstream scripts

file.content:
# ADR: transform_edm_to_mocho.py ã Implementation Decisions

**Date**: 2026-04-14  
**Status**: Accepted  
**Related**: `transform-adr.md` (D0 ã design), `transform-props-mapping-adr.md` (property decisions ã D6/D7/D8 moved there), `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md`

---

## Context

This document records implementation decisions for `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`,
the reference DDB-EDM JSONL ã mocho RDF transform. Dispatch architecture and
signal priority are decided in `transform-adr.md` (D0); the decisions here
govern how those design choices are realised in code.

---

## Decision 1: Use JSONL as input, not the NT file

**Decision**: Stream `items-all-goethe-faust.json` (JSONL, one JSON object per
line). The NT file `ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt` is not used.

**Alternatives considered**:
- *NT file*: Load triples from the pre-generated NT and align by predicate IRI.
  Rejected because: (a) the NT was generated from the same JSONL and carries no
  additional information; (b) the alignment table is keyed by `(entity_type,
  json_key)` matching the JSON structure, not by IRI, so NT input would require
  a reverse IRIãjson_key lookup that is neither defined nor stable.

**Rationale**: JSONL streams with constant memory, preserves the `(entity_type,
json_key)` lookup key structure, and requires only stdlib `json`. No NT indexing
phase needed.

---

## Decision 2: stdlib only ã no rdflib in the transform script

**Decision**: The transform script uses only Python stdlib (`json`, `csv`, `re`,
`collections`, `argparse`, `pathlib`). rdflib is not imported.

**Rationale**: The transform is a data pipeline step, not an ontology processing
step. N-Triples output can be constructed by string formatting. rdflib adds a
non-stdlib dependency and significant startup overhead for a script that runs
over 115k records.

**Scope**: This applies only to `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`. Upstream scripts
that parse OWL or Turtle (`align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`, `gen_htype_doco_mapping.py`)
continue to use rdflib.

---

## Decision 3: Dispatch signal field paths and htype lookup key

**Decision**: The four dispatch signals are read from the following JSONL paths:

| Signal | JSONL path | Value example | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| sector | `provider-info.domains[0]` | `http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte001` | Vocnet sparte IRI; `domains[0]` is always the primary sector |
| mediatype | `edm.RDF.WebResource[0].type.resource` | `http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt002` | Vocnet medientyp IRI; taken from first WebResource |
| htype | `edm.RDF.ProvidedCHO.hierarchyType` | `"htype_030"` | Literal `htype_code` string; absent if record has no hierarchy type |

---

## Plan: deduplicate shared PROV-O node triples

### What's redundant

Four nodes in `emit_prov_triples` have stable URIs that recur across records. Their descriptive triples are re-emitted identically for every record:

| Node | URI | # triples duplicated | Scope |
|---|---|---|---|
| DDB Agent | `DDB_BASE` (fixed) | 3 | entire run |
| XSLT SoftwareAgent | `urn:ãÎ:mapping-version:<ver>` | 3 | all records with same `mapping-version` |
| Dataset | `urn:ãÎ:dataset-id:<id>` | 3ã5 | all records in same dataset |
| Provider Agent | `urn:ãÎ:provider-ddb-id:<id>` | 4ã5 | all records from same provider |

The per-CHO linking triples (`prov:wasAttributedTo`, `prov:wasDerivedFrom`, etc.) are unique per record and must NOT be deduplicated.

---

### DuckDB ã not needed

The run is a single-process, single-pass stream. An in-memory `set[str]` of already-emitted URIs is sufficient ã O(1) lookup, zero dependencies. DuckDB would only be needed for cross-worker or cross-run deduplication, neither of which applies here. The existing DuckDB in `__main__.py` is write-only werk_staging and is unrelated.

---

### Code changes (3 files)

**`emitters.py`** ã `emit_prov_triples` gets a new optional `emitted: set[str] | None = None` parameter. Each of the four shared-node blocks is wrapped:

```python
if emitted is not None and uri in emitted:
    pass  # skip descriptive triples; linking triples from CHO already emitted above
else:
    if emitted is not None:
        emitted.add(uri)
    # ... descriptive triple lines ...
```

**`transform.py`** ã `transform_record` gets `emitted_prov: set[str] | None = None`, passes it through to `emit_prov_triples(record, ddb_uri, GRAPH_PROV, emitted=emitted_prov)`.

**`__main__.py`** ã one line before the record loop: `emitted_prov: set[str] = set()`. Passed into every `transform_record(...)` call.

---

### Notes changes (2 files)

**`ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md` ôÏ2.1** ã add a "Shared node deduplication" note after the five-node table: shared nodes (DDB Agent, XSLT, Dataset, Provider) emit descriptive triples only on first encounter per run; the CHO linking triples are always emitted.

**`transform-script-adr.md`** ã add **Decision 29**: in-memory `set` for shared PROV-O node deduplication. Document: (a) which four nodes are guarded, (b) why not DuckDB (single-pass, single-process), (c) `emitted` is `None`-safe so callers that don't pass it get the current re-emit behavior.

---

Ready to implement?

Updated plan:

---

## Plan (revised): DuckDB-backed cross-run PROV-O deduplication

### Mechanism

A shared, persistent DuckDB file (separate from the per-run `werk-staging.duckdb`) stores a single table of already-emitted shared-node URIs. Each run:
1. opens the file and loads existing URIs into memory as `set[str]` (the number of unique providers/datasets/XSLT versions is small ã tens to hundreds)
2. uses that set during processing to suppress redundant descriptive triples
3. writes newly-emitted URIs back to DuckDB at the end of the run via `INSERT OR IGNORE`

### Schema (one table, fixed path)
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS emitted_prov_uris (uri VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY)
```

### Code changes (3 files)

**`emitters.py`** ã same as before: `emit_prov_triples` gets `emitted: set[str] | None = None`; each of the four shared-node blocks is guarded by `if emitted is None or uri not in emitted`; new URIs are `emitted.add(uri)` on first emit. `None` falls back to re-emit every time (safe default).

**`transform.py`** ã `transform_record` gets `emitted_prov: set[str] | None = None`, threads it into `emit_prov_triples`.

**`__main__.py`** ã three additions:
- New CLI arg: `--prov-db PATH` ã path to the shared dedup DuckDB; optional; if omitted, fresh in-memory set is used (within-run dedup only, no persistence)
- At startup (after the existing DuckDB `conn` setup): open `--prov-db`, create table if absent, load all rows into `emitted_prov: set[str]`
- After the record loop closes (before `conn.close()`): `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO emitted_prov_uris` for all URIs in `emitted_prov`

### Notes changes (2 files)

**`ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md` ôÏ2.1** ã add "Shared node deduplication" paragraph after the five-node table.

**`transform-script-adr.md`** ã add **Decision 29**: DuckDB-backed cross-run dedup for shared PROV-O nodes. Document: which four nodes are guarded, why DuckDB (multi-run, separate sector files), why load-into-memory rather than per-record DB lookup (provider/dataset count is small; avoids DB round-trip per record), `None`-safe fallback.

---

## Plan (final): shared-entity deduplication via DuckDB

### Schema ã one table, shared DuckDB file

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS emitted_entities (
    uri         VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
    entity_type VARCHAR NOT NULL
)
```

`entity_type` values: `prov_xslt`, `prov_ddb`, `prov_provider`, `prov_dataset`, `edm_agent`, `edm_place`, `skos_concept`, `edm_timespan`, `rights`

**XSLT note**: the lookup key in code is `xslt_uri` derived from `map_ver` (`urn:ddbedm:properties:mapping-version:{ver}`). The version number is what's in the JSON; the URI is computed from it ã so "version number is the key" holds at the code level; the table stores the derived URI as with all other entries.

### Runtime pattern per run

- Load all existing `uri` values into a `dict[str, str]` (`uri ã entity_type`) at startup ã doubles as O(1) lookup and write-back source
- Pass this dict into every `transform_record()` call
- At end: `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO emitted_entities SELECT * FROM VALUES (?, ?)` for all entries in the dict ã idempotent, handles pre-existing entries safely

---

**`emitters.py`**
- `emit_prov_triples(record, ddb_cho_uri, graph_iri, emitted=None)` ã guard XSLT, DDB Agent, Provider Agent, Dataset blocks: skip descriptive triples if URI already in `emitted`; add `(uri, entity_type)` on first emit
- `emit_ddbedm_triples(rdf, graph_iri, lang_coll=None, emitted=None)` ã guard descriptive triple blocks for `edm:Agent`, `edm:Place`, `skos:Concept`, `edm:TimeSpan`, and rights URIs similarly

**`transform.py`**
- `transform_record(..., emitted_entities=None)` ã pass through to both emitters

**`__main__.py`**
- New CLI arg: `--entities-db PATH` (optional; if omitted, fresh in-memory dict, within-run dedup only)
- Startup: open DuckDB at `--entities-db`, `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`, load rows into `emitted_entities: dict[str, str]`
- Record loop: pass `emitted_entities` to every `transform_record()`
- After loop: batch `INSERT OR IGNORE` all entries back to DuckDB; close connection

---

### Notes changes (2 files)

**`ddbedm-prov-o-plan.md` ôÏ2.1** ã add "Shared node deduplication" note after the five-node table covering: which nodes are guarded, that dedup is cross-run via DuckDB, XSLT key derivation

**`transform-script-adr.md` ã Decision 29**: DuckDB-backed shared-entity dedup. Cover: which 9 entity types, one-table design rationale, `dict[str, str]` as both lookup and write-back, `--entities-db` optional flag, `None`-safe fallback

1 # Transform implementation ã as-built 2 3 **Date**: 2026-05-06 4 **Status**: Implemented and verified on full goethe-faust corpus (115,432 records) 5 **Package**: `scripts/transform/` (invoked as `python -m transform` from `scripts/`) 6 7 Reference for the original design intent: `notes/transform-implementation-plan.md`. 8 Validation findings: `notes/transform-validation.md`. 9 Full-corpus run planning: `notes/transform-dryrun-plan.md`. 10 11 --- 12 13 ## 1. Package structure 14 15 The original plan targeted a monolithic rewrite of `scripts/transform_edm_to_mocho.py`. The actual implementation refactored into a Python package: 16 17 | Module | Contents | 18 |---|---| 19 | `constants.py` | IRIs, prefix table (`_PREFIXES`), dispatch tables, path defaults, type aliases | 20 | `utils.py` | N-Quads formatting, URI minting, IRI sanitisation, value normalisation | 21 | `loaders.py` | CSV/JSON config loaders | 22 | `emitters.py` | Triple emitters: ddbedm passthrough, mocho alignment, PROV-O, werk_staging | 23 | `transform.py` | `transform_record` ã per-record orchestration | 24 | `__main__.py` | CLI entry point | 25 26 --- 27 28 ## 2. Deviations from the plan 29 30 ### 2.1 No in-process multiprocessing 31 32 The plan (ôÏ8) included `--workers N` and `--batch-size N` for a `ProcessPoolExecutor` approach. This was dropped in favour of **Option C parallel** (`transform-dryrun-plan.md ôÏ5.2`): export per-sector JSONL files from SQLite once, then run one `python -m transform` worker per sector as separate OS processes. No code changes required in the transform itself. 33 34 Rationale: per-UID SQLite random lookups are slower than sequential JSONL reads at 18.5M scale; sector split is the natural parallelism boundary; transform output is already sharded by sector. See `transform-dryrun-plan.md ôÏ5` for the full trade-off table. 35 36 ### 2.2 CLI flags ã additions and removals 37 38 **Planned but not implemented:** 39 - `--workers N`, `--batch-size N` ã dropped (Option C) 40 - `--out` ã output is always `/goethe-faust.nq`; no free-choice path flag 41 - `--werk-staging` ã DuckDB path is always `/goethe-faust-werk-staging.duckdb` 42 43 **Added (not in plan):** 44 - `--total N` ã expected total records; enables ETA in progress log 45 - `--log-interval N` ã log a progress line every N records (default: 100,000) 46 - SIGINT/SIGTERM signal handler ã graceful exit after current record; partial stats and errors written 47 48 **Output directory:** auto-timestamped `output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS/` rather than fixed paths. Override with `--outdir`. 49 50 ### 2.3 Stats expansion 51 52 The plan's `--stats` levels were implemented as designed. Additionally, during development the following were added at `dispatch` level (all from emitter Counters ã no post-hoc N-Quad scanning): 53 54 - `records.by_mediatype` ã mediatype distribution (short codes: `mt001`ã`mt007`) 55 - `records.by_htype` ã htype distribution (short codes: `ht021`, etc.) 56 - `records.uri_sanitized` ã IRIs percent-encoded due to illegal characters (RFC 3987) 57 - `records.uri_split` ã individual URIs extracted from multi-URI `resource` fields 58 - `records.uri_about_split` ã extra `owl:sameAs` triples for multi-URI `about` fields 59 - `ddbedm_classes` ã entity class instance counts in the ddbedm graph 60 - `ddbedm_vocab.properties_all` ã predicate counts for the ddbedm stream 61 - `mocho_vocab.properties_all` / `properties_new` ã predicate counts for the mocho stream 62 63 `full` level now aliases `dispatch` (reserved for future additions; the former regex-based predicate extraction was removed). 64 65 Schema and field rationale: `notes/transform-stats-plan.md`. 66 67 ### 2.4 URI sanitisation and multi-URI data quality fixes 68 69 Not in the original plan. Added to `utils.py`: 70 71 **`_sanitize_iri(iri)`** ã percent-encodes characters illegal in N-Triples IRI references (`[\x00-\x20<>"{}|\\^\x7f]`). Ported from `gemea/scripts/py/export_ddb.py`. Applied inside `value_to_nt_obj` for every `{"resource": ...}` value. 72 73 **Multi-URI `resource` fields** ã 1,250 fields in the goethe-faust corpus contain multiple space-separated URIs in a single `resource` value (e.g. `"http://www.geonames.org/2856883 http://vocab.getty.edu/tgn/7005252"`). `value_to_nt_obj` splits on whitespace and emits each URI as a separate triple. 74 75 **Multi-URI `about` fields** ã 1,178 entities (`Place`, `WebResource`, `Agent`) have multiple space-separated URIs in `about`. `emit_ddbedm_triples` uses the first URI as the RDF subject and emits `owl:sameAs` for each additional URI (+1,309 triples in the goethe-faust corpus). 76 77 --- 78 79 ## 3. Actual CLI 80 81 Run from `scripts/` directory: 82 83 ```bash 84 python -m transform [OPTIONS] 85 ``` 86 87 ### I/O 88 89 | Flag | Default | Description | 90 |---|---|---| 91 | `--jsonl FILE` | `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` | JSONL input (one DDB-EDM JSON object per line) | 92 | `--ids FILE\|-` | _(none)_ | ID allowlist file, or `-` for stdin; omit to process all | 93 | `--outdir DIR` | `output/transform/YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS` | Output directory; auto-timestamped if omitted | 94 95 ### Config 96 97 | Flag | Default | 98 |---|---| 99 | `--alignment FILE` | `output/config/lookup_class_prop_alignment.csv` | 100 | `--lido FILE` | `output/config/lido_event_types.csv` | 101 | `--htype FILE` | `output/config/lookup_htype_doco_rico.csv` | 102 | `--mediatype FILE` | `output/config/lookup_mediatype_class.csv` | 103 | `--audio FILE` | `output/config/audio_type2class.json` | 104 105 ### Run control 106 107 | Flag | Default | Description | 108 |---|---|---| 109 | `--stats none\|basic\|dispatch\|full` | `basic` | Stats verbosity in `transform_stats.json` | 110 | `--log-level DEBUG\|INFO\|WARNING\|ERROR` | `INFO` | Log verbosity | 111 | `--limit N` | _(none)_ | Stop after N records (smoke-testing) | 112 | `--total N` | _(none)_ | Expected total records ã enables ETA in progress log | 113 | `--log-interval N` | `100000` | Progress log line every N records | 114 | `--debug` | ã | Shorthand for `--log-level DEBUG` | 115 116 --- 117 118 ## 4. Output files 119 120 Each invocation derives the output filename stem from the input: for SQLite input `s2.sqlite` with `--offset N` the stem is `s2-N`; for JSONL input `foo.json` the stem is `foo`. Override with `--stem`. 121 122 Single-worker output: 123 ``` 124 / 125 .nq N-Quads, all named graphs 126 -werk-staging.duckdb W-slot staging rows 127 -stats.json run statistics 128 -errors.jsonl per-record errors (written live) 129 .log run log 130 ``` 131 132 In the production parallel-sector run (`run-transform-sector.sh`), all workers for one sector share a single `nq/` shard directory; merged outputs land one level up named by sector: 133 ``` 134 / 135 nq/ 136 s2-0.nq s2-0-stats.json s2-0.log ... ã worker shards (deleted after merge) 137 s2-38400.nq ... 138 ... 139 s2.nq merged N-Quads (--merge-all only) 140 s2-werk-staging.duckdb merged werk_staging 141 s2-stats.json merged stats 142 s2-errors.jsonl merged errors 143 s2.log merged log 144 ``` 145 146 Running sectors sequentially into the same `/` dir produces non-colliding `s1-*` and `s2-*` files. 147 148 Named graphs: 149 150 | Graph IRI | Content | 151 |---|---| 152 | `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/ddbedm` | Verbatim EDM passthrough (always, including mt007) | 153 | `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/mocho` | mocho-aligned triples (skipped for mt007) | 154 | `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/prov` | PROV-O Layer 1 (always) | 155 | `https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/lang-title` | `dcterms:language` provenance triples for normalized lang codes (emitted only when a record carries at least one invalid BCP 47 code) | 156 157 --- 158 159 ## 5. POC run ã goethe-faust reference corpus (2026-05-06) 160 161 | Metric | Value | 162 |---|---| 163 | Records processed | 115,432 | 164 | Triples total | 14,713,376 | 165 | ddbedm / mocho / prov | 8,957,262 / 1,898,754 / 3,857,360 | 166 | werk_staging rows | 15 (`rdac:C10001` only) | 167 | Errors (parse + transform) | 0 | 168 | fallback_d9 | 0 | 169 | uri_split | 4,188 | 170 | uri_about_split | 1,309 | 171 | uri_sanitized | 29 | 172 173 Dispatch sum check: 25,644 (htype) + 47,428 (mediatype) + 0 (fallback) + 42,360 (mt007) = 115,432 ã 174 175 --- 176 177 ## 6. Open issues from validation 178 179 From `notes/transform-validation.md`: 180 181 ### 6.1 `mocho:ImageWork` missing from werk_staging 182 183 `_W_SLOT_CLASSES` triggers on `rdac:C10001` and `mo:MusicalWork` only. `transform-revised-plan.md ôÏ1.1` marks `mocho:ImageWork` as a GND-Werk target (ht015 Illustration, ht019 Karte, sparte005 mt002). No `mocho:ImageWork` records in the goethe-faust corpus, so no current impact. **Fix before running on a broader corpus.** 184 185 ### 6.2 `ec:EditorialWork` werk_staging scope 186 187 Adding `ec:EditorialWork` would produce 88 additional staging rows (mt005 Video). `transform-revised-plan.md ôÏ1.1` has no "GND Werk" column entry for mt005/EditorialWork. Video productions do not have GND Werk authority records. Left out intentionally; revisit when GND linking scope is finalised. 188 189 --- 190 191 ## 7. Emitter safety audit (2026-05-07) 192 193 ### 7.1 Issue categories 194 195 Three systemic gaps (AãC) and one feature addition (D): 196 197 | # | Category | Root cause / motivation | Emitters affected | 198 |---|---|---|---| 199 | A | `
` in literals | `_escape_literal` does not normalize HTML line-break tags; the unescaped tag appears verbatim in the N-Quad literal | Any emitter that calls `_escape_literal` on a field containing `
` | 200 | B | Multi-URI `resource` not split | Emitters that manually extract `val.get("resource")` treat space-separated URIs as one string, producing a malformed IRI | `emit_subject_triples`, `emit_hastype_triples`, `emit_creator_triples`, `emit_contributor_triples`, `emit_aggregation_triples`, `emit_place_stubs` | 201 | C | Bare IDs not expanded in special emitters | Same emitters bypass `expand_obj_nt`; also `emit_prov_triples` passes `provider_isil` without `_sanitize_iri` | `emit_creator_triples`, `emit_contributor_triples`, `emit_place_stubs`, `emit_prov_triples` | 202 | D | `edm:currentLocation` ã IRI-with-label-stub | Currently emitted via generic loop (no label stub). Should follow the same "IRI-with-label-stub" pattern as `edm:hasType`: URI values get a `rdfs:label` stub from the matching `edm:Place`; literal values pass through unchanged | New `emit_current_location_triples`; `"currentLocation"` added to `_MOCHO_SKIP` | 203 204 Note: `value_to_nt_obj` (used by the generic loops in `emit_ddbedm_triples` and `emit_mocho_triples`) already handles B and C correctly via `.split()` and `_sanitize_iri`. The gaps are exclusively in special-case emitters that extract `resource` manually. 205 206 Corpus evidence (from `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`): 207 - **B/D** ã `23MBRVVDUHBFZNDCTK4SQM7KROKGVGNF`: `ProvidedCHO.currentLocation.resource = "http://d-nb.info/gnd/4044283-4 https://www.geonames.org/2855745"` and matching `Place.about` 208 - **A** ã `223GMAWUHPGI76OQUKSL54XVOCHHXDWD`: description field contains `"...1749, +22. MûÊrz 1832
Eduard Lassen..."` 209 - **C** ã `222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL`: `ProvidedCHO.hasType[0].resource = "DJVX2BT7X2HN24O6YRDOQM6T3CNZYYY6"` (bare 32-char ID) 210 211 ### 7.2 Design ã `resource_uris()` utility 212 213 Rather than patching each emitter individually, one utility in `utils.py` encapsulates the three sub-steps every manual `resource` extraction must perform: 214 215 ```python 216 def resource_uris( 217 resource_raw: str, 218 bare_id_to_uri: dict[str, str] | None = None, 219 entity_class: str = "Agent", 220 ) -> list[str]: 221 """Expand, sanitize, and split all URIs from a (possibly multi-value) resource string. 222 223 Steps: (1) split on whitespace; (2) expand bare IDs via index or mint_bare_id fallback; 224 (3) percent-encode unsafe characters. Returns [] for empty input. 225 """ 226 ``` 227 228 Callers pass `(val.get("resource") or "").strip()`, the per-record `bare_id_to_uri` index, and the entity class for bare-ID minting. Returns a list of safe, full URI strings ready for `f"<{uri}>"` wrapping. 229 230 **Primary-URI rule**: emitters that need the raw first URI before expansion (e.g. `event_participant_index` lookup in `emit_contributor_triples`, `resolve_agent` in `emit_creator_triples`) extract `resource_raw.split()[0]` before calling `resource_uris()`. 231 232 ### 7.3 Change inventory 233 234 **`utils.py`** 235 236 | Change | Detail | 237 |---|---| 238 | Add `_BR_RE` | `re.compile(r'', re.IGNORECASE)` | 239 | Update `_escape_literal` | Prepend `s = _BR_RE.sub('\n', s)` before the escape chain | 240 | Add `resource_uris()` | New utility; imported by `emitters.py` | 241 242 **`emitters.py`** 243 244 | Emitter | Change | 245 |---|---| 246 | `emit_subject_triples` | Replace single-URI `resource` branch with `resource_uris(resource_raw, _bare, "Concept")` loop | 247 | `emit_hastype_triples` | Same pattern | 248 | `emit_creator_triples` | Add `bare_id_to_uri=None` param; Track 1: `resource_uris()` loop; Track 2: `resource_raw.split()[0]` for `resolve_agent`; apply `_sanitize_iri(agent_uri)` | 249 | `emit_contributor_triples` | Add `bare_id_to_uri=None` param; `resource_raw.split()[0]` for `event_participant_index` lookup; `resource_uris()` loop for triples | 250 | `emit_prov_triples` | Apply `_sanitize_iri(provider_isil)` | 251 | `emit_place_stubs` | Split `raw_about`; pass only first part to `mint_bare_id` with `_sanitize_iri` | 252 | `emit_aggregation_triples` | Inline split+sanitize loop for `isShownAt.resource`, `dataProvider.resource`, `object.resource` (no bare ID expansion ã aggregation URIs are always full) | 253 | `emit_mocho_triples` | Pass `bare_id_to_uri` to `emit_creator_triples` and `emit_contributor_triples` | 254 255 **`emitters.py` ã imports**: add `resource_uris` to `from .utils import`. 256 257 ### 7.4 Fixture-based integration tests 258 259 Three real corpus records are saved to `scripts/transform/tests/fixtures/` as minimal inspection targets. Each record is stored as `.json` (single-record JSON, not JSONL). 260 261 | File | Record ID | Pattern | 262 |---|---|---| 263 | `fixtures/multi_uri.json` | `23MBRVVDUHBFZNDCTK4SQM7KROKGVGNF` | Multi-URI in `Place.about` and `currentLocation.resource` | 264 | `fixtures/br_tag.json` | `223GMAWUHPGI76OQUKSL54XVOCHHXDWD` | `
` in description literal | 265 | `fixtures/bare_id.json` | `222NZKK63TNRLC2VETRV722VKBDSUVGL` | Bare 32-char ID in `hasType.resource` | 266 267 After fixes are applied, a fixture script `tests/make_fixtures.py` runs the full transform on all three records and writes `fixtures/.nq` ã the complete N-Quads output for human inspection. 268 269 Integration tests in `test_transform.py` (new `TestFixtures` class) load each `.json`, call `transform_record()`, and make targeted assertions: 270 271 | Test | Assertion | 272 |---|---| 273 | `test_multi_uri_place_splits` | Two separate `Place` subject IRIs emitted; no IRI containing a space | 274 | `test_multi_uri_current_location_splits` | Two separate triples for `currentLocation` | 275 | `test_br_tag_normalized` | `\\n` appears in the relevant literal; no ``, `
`, `
` all produce `\\n` | 283 | `TestResourceUris` | empty ã `[]`; single full URI ã `[sanitized]`; two space-separated ã two entries; bare ID ã index lookup; bare ID fallback ã `mint_bare_id` (`urn:ddbedm:`) | 284 | `TestEmitSubjectTriplesMultiUri` | `"URI1 URI2"` ã two `dcterms:subject` triples | 285 | `TestEmitHastypeTriplesMultiUri` | Same for `edm:hasType` | 286 | `TestEmitCreatorTriplesMultiUri` | Two URIs ã two Track-1 triples | 287 | `TestEmitCreatorTriplesBareId` | Bare ID expanded; `agent_uri` sanitized | 288 | `TestEmitContributorTriplesMultiUri` | Two URIs ã two `(cho, prop, uri)` triples | 289 | `TestEmitContributorTriplesBareId` | Bare ID expanded via param | 290 | `TestEmitProvTriplesIsil` | `provider_isil` with unsafe chars ã sanitized in `MOCHO_ISIL` triple | 291 | `TestEmitPlaceStubsSplitAbout` | Space-separated `about` ã only first part used as subject | 292 | `TestEmitAggregationSplitUri` | `isShownAt.resource = "URI1 URI2"` ã two `dcterms:source` triples | 293 294 ### 7.6 Validation run ã goethe-faust corpus (2026-05-07) 295 296 Full run on `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` (115,432 records) after all audit fixes. Output: `output/transform/20260507_190805/`. 297 298 | Metric | POC (2026-05-06) | Post-audit (2026-05-07) | Delta | 299 |---|---|---|---| 300 | Records processed | 115,432 | 115,432 | ã | 301 | Triples total | 14,713,376 | 14,764,352 | +50,976 | 302 | ddbedm | 8,957,262 | 8,957,734 | +472 | 303 | **mocho** | **1,898,754** | **1,950,504** | **+51,750** | 304 | prov | 3,857,360 | 3,856,114 | ã1,246 | 305 | Errors | 0 | 0 | ã | 306 | fallback_d9 | 0 | 0 | ã | 307 | uri_sanitized | 29 | 29 | ã | 308 | uri_split | 4,188 | 2,685 | ã1,503 | 309 | uri_about_split | 1,309 | 1,309 | ã | 310 311 **mocho +51,750** breaks down as: 312 - `edm:currentLocation` ã 31,837 new triples (property moved from generic loop to `emit_current_location_triples`; IRI-with-label-stub pattern) 313 - Creator/contributor bare-ID expansions, multi-URI splits, and subject/hasType label stubs now handled correctly in special emitters account for the remainder 314 315 **uri_split ã1,503**: some multi-URI splits previously counted via the generic loop (`value_to_nt_obj`) are now handled in special emitters via `resource_uris()` and tracked separately; the net split count is lower because `currentLocation` URIs (many multi-value) are no longer double-counted. 316 317 **prov ã1,246**: minor change due to PROV provider node deduplication; no logic change ã variance from record ordering in input. 318 319 --- 320 321 ## 8. Agent label stub fixes (2026-05-08) 322 323 Two bugs in `emitters.py` prevented `rdfs:label` from being emitted on agent stubs in the mocho graph. Both deviate from the spec in `transform-props-mapping-plan.md ôÏ4ã5` ("Label sourced from `edm:Agent.prefLabel[].$` (first value). Applies to both creator and contributor URI resolutions."). 324 325 ### 8.1 Bug 1 ã `emit_creator_triples`: `isinstance(pref, str)` always False 326 327 Track 2 of `emit_creator_triples` emits `dcterms:creator ` + `mocho:Agent` type when `resolve_agent` finds a matching `edm:Agent`. It also should emit `rdfs:label` sourced from `agent.prefLabel[].$`. The pre-fix code: 328 329 ```python 330 pref = agent.get("prefLabel") or label 331 if pref and isinstance(pref, str): # always False ã prefLabel is a list 332 lines.append(make_nq(agent_nt, RDFS_LABEL, f'"{_escape_literal(pref)}"', graph_iri)) 333 ``` 334 335 `agent.get("prefLabel")` returns a list (e.g. `[{"$": "Goethe, ãÎ", "lang": "de"}]`). `isinstance(list, str)` is always `False`, so no `rdfs:label` was ever emitted from the agent's own prefLabel. When the list was empty, the fallback `or label` set `pref` to the literal string from the creator field ã which did pass `isinstance(str)` ã but that path is rare and emits without lang-tag. 336 337 **Fix**: replace with `coerce_list(agent.get("prefLabel"))` + `value_to_nt_obj` iteration (same pattern as `emit_hastype_triples`); fall back to the `label` string only when prefLabel is empty. 338 339 ### 8.2 Bug 2 ã `emit_contributor_triples`: no `agents_index`, label from `val.get("$")` 340 341 `emit_contributor_triples` had no `agents_index` parameter, so: 342 343 1. **URI case**: `rdfs:label` was sourced from `val.get("$")` ã the literal annotation on the contributor field ã not from the matching `edm:Agent.prefLabel`. 344 2. **Literal case**: no `resolve_agent` call at all. Contributor literals that matched a DDB/GND agent by label were emitted as plain `dc:contributor "literal"@lang` instead of an agent stub. 345 346 **Fix**: add `agents_index: dict[str, AgentDict] | None = None` parameter. 347 - **URI case**: `resolve_agent("", primary_resource, _agents)` ã use `prefLabel` from agent; fall back to `val.get("$")` if not found. 348 - **Literal case**: `resolve_agent(label, "", _agents)` ã if DDB/GND match, emit ` ` + `mocho:Agent` + `rdfs:label`; else emit plain literal. 349 350 Update `emit_mocho_triples` call site to pass `agents_index`. 351 352 ### 8.3 Test additions 353 354 Eight new tests in `scripts/transform/tests/test_transform.py`: 355 356 | Class | Test | Covers | 357 |---|---|---| 358 | `TestEmitCreatorTriplesPrefLabel` | `test_preflabel_list_dict_emitted` | prefLabel list-of-dicts ã label emitted | 359 | | `test_preflabel_lang_tagged` | lang tag preserved (`"Schiller, Friedrich"@de`) | 360 | | `test_preflabel_empty_list_falls_back_to_label` | empty prefLabel ã fallback to literal | 361 | | `test_uri_track2_uses_agents_index_preflabel` | URI path also uses agents_index prefLabel | 362 | `TestEmitContributorTriplesAgentLabel` | `test_uri_case_uses_agents_index_preflabel` | agents_index prefLabel wins over `val["$"]` | 363 | | `test_uri_case_fallback_to_literal_label_when_no_index` | no agents_index ã `val["$"]` used | 364 | | `test_literal_match_emits_agent_stub` | literal matching DDB/GND agent ã stub emitted | 365 | | `test_literal_no_match_emits_plain_literal` | unmatched literal ã plain `dc:contributor` | 366 367 Total: 114 tests (106 post-audit + 8 new). 368 369 ### 8.4 Validation run ã goethe-faust corpus (2026-05-08) 370 371 Full run on `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json` (115,432 records) after agent label fixes. Output: `output/transform/20260507_232804/`. 372 373 | Metric | Pre-fix (20260507_190805) | Post-fix (20260507_232804) | ö | 374 |---|---|---|---| 375 | Records processed | 115,432 | 115,432 | ã | 376 | Triples total | 14,764,352 | 14,782,653 | **+18,301** | 377 | ddbedm | 8,957,734 | 8,957,734 | 0 | 378 | **mocho** | **1,950,504** | **1,968,805** | **+18,301** | 379 | prov | 3,856,114 | 3,856,114 | 0 | 380 | `rdfs:label` (mocho) | 302,578 | 320,754 | **+18,176** | 381 | `rdf:type` (mocho) | 162,807 | 162,932 | **+125** | 382 | `dcterms:creator` | 53,453 | 53,453 | 0 | 383 | `dc:contributor` | 36,773 | 36,773 | 0 | 384 385 18,176 + 125 = 18,301 ã all new mocho triples accounted for by the two fixes. 386 387 **Attribution:** 388 - **+18,176 `rdfs:label`** ã Bug 1. Creator Track 2 now correctly iterates `agent.prefLabel[]` via `value_to_nt_obj`; all previously silenced agent labels are emitted. The contributor literal-match branch contributes its `rdfs:label` half here as well. 389 - **+125 `rdf:type mocho:Agent`** ã Bug 2 (literal-match path). Contributor literals that previously fell through as plain `dc:contributor "ãÎ"@lang` now resolve to DDB/GND agents and emit `mocho:Agent` type stubs. 390 - `dcterms:creator` and `dc:contributor` counts unchanged ã the fixes add stubs for existing agent resolutions, not new CHO-level predicates. 391 392 --- 393 394 ## 9. `hierarchyType` URI fixes (2026-05-12) 395 396 Two bugs in `retype_entities` (`emitters.py`) in the handling of `ProvidedCHO.hierarchyType`. 397 398 Corpus evidence: object `LH3LUU63TUKZJHMQEILGTP3HZXZP5IFT` (`s2.sqlite`) has `"hierarchyType": "htype_007 htype_020"`. 399 400 ### 9.1 Bug 1 ã wrong vocnet URI form 401 402 Raw values use the form `htype_NNN` (e.g. `htype_007`). The correct vocnet IRI suffix is `htNNN` (e.g. `ht007`). The emitter was appending the raw code directly to `_HTYPE_PREFIX`, producing the invalid IRI `http://ddb.vocnet.org/hierarchietyp/htype_007`. 403 404 **Fix** (`emitters.py` lines 277ã283): apply `.replace("htype_", "ht")` to each code before constructing the IRI. 405 406 ### 9.2 Bug 2 ã space-separated multi-value treated as single code 407 408 `hierarchyType` can contain multiple space-separated codes. The emitter passed the raw string as one unit, producing a single IRI with an embedded space: `<ãÎ/htype_007 htype_020>`. The dispatch lookup (`htype_map.get(htype_code)`) also silently failed for any multi-value record. 409 410 **Fix** (`emitters.py`): 411 - **Dispatch** (layer 1): `htype_map.get(htype_code.split()[0])` ã first code only, consistent with "most specific class" intent. 412 - **URI emission**: iterate `htype_code.split()`; emit one `ddbedm:hierarchyType` triple per code with the corrected IRI. 413 414 ### 9.3 Test additions 415 416 Three new tests in `TestRetypeEntities` (`test_transform.py`): 417 418 | Test | Assertion | 419 |---|---| 420 | `test_multi_htype_emits_two_triples` | `"htype_007 htype_020"` ã exactly 2 `ddbedm:hierarchyType` triples, IRIs `ht007` and `ht020` | 421 | `test_multi_htype_no_space_in_iri` | No object token in any `ddbedm:hierarchyType` triple contains a space | 422 | `test_multi_htype_dispatch_uses_first` | `flags["htype_used"] is True` and `flags["fallback"] is False` ã first code resolves a class | 423 424 Two existing tests updated: `test_sparte001_mt003_htype021` and `test_htype_emitted_as_iri` now assert `ht021`/`ht042` (not `htype_021`/`htype_042`) in the emitted IRI. 425 426 Total: 140 tests. 427 428 --- 429 430 ## 10. BCP 47 language tag normalization (2026-05-12) 431 432 QLever validates RDF language tags against BCP 47 and terminates indexing on the first invalid tag. DDB source records carry ISO 639-2 collective codes (e.g. `wen`, `gem`) and malformed codes (e.g. `gerger`) in the `lang` field of cortex JSON. Neither is a valid BCP 47 individual-language subtag. 433 434 Design record: `gemea/notes/ingest/transform-language-tag.md`. 435 436 ### 10.1 Validation approach 437 438 `langcodes` (v3.5.1, added to `requirements.txt`) is used rather than a hand-curated dict. Two failure classes must be caught: 439 440 1. **Malformed codes** (e.g. `gerger`) ã `langcodes.tag_is_valid()` returns `False`. 441 2. **IANA collection subtags** (e.g. `wen`, `gem`) ã `langcodes.tag_is_valid()` returns `True` (they are in the IANA registry), but QLever rejects them. Detected by membership in `_IANA_COLLECTION_CODES`, a `frozenset` parsed at import time from the IANA registry bundled with `langcodes` (`data/language-subtag-registry.txt`, `Scope: collection` entries). 116 codes in the current registry. 442 443 Both ã normalized to `"und"` (BCP 47 "undetermined"). Original code retained via provenance triple. 444 445 ### 10.2 Code changes 446 447 **`utils.py`** 448 449 | Change | Detail | 450 |---|---| 451 | `_build_iana_collection_codes()` | Parses bundled IANA registry line-by-line; returns `frozenset` of all `Scope: collection` subtags. Falls back to empty set on error. | 452 | `_IANA_COLLECTION_CODES` | Module-level `frozenset` built at import. | 453 | `_invalid_bcp47(lang)` | `@lru_cache(maxsize=512)`. `True` if `not tag_is_valid(lang) or lang in _IANA_COLLECTION_CODES`. | 454 | `value_to_nt_obj()` | Added `lang_coll: set[str] \| None = None` parameter. Strips `lang` before validation; guards `lang_coll.add()` against values containing internal spaces (see ôÏ10.3). | 455 456 **`emitters.py`** 457 458 `lang_coll: set[str] | None = None` added to `emit_ddbedm_triples()` and `emit_mocho_triples()`; passed to every `value_to_nt_obj()` call that processes CHO fields (title, generic property loop). Secondary emitters (`emit_subject_triples`, `emit_creator_triples`, `emit_contributor_triples`, `emit_hastype_triples`, `emit_current_location_triples`) are unchanged ã corpus analysis (`analyse_lang_tags_by_entity.py`) confirmed no collective codes on non-CHO entities. 459 460 **`transform.py`** 461 462 `lang_coll: set[str] = set()` created in `transform_record()`; passed to both emitters (mutated in place). After emitters complete, one `dcterms:language ` provenance triple per unique original code is emitted to `GRAPH_LANG_TITLE`. Subject is `ddb_uri` (not `cho_uri`). 463 464 **`constants.py`** 465 466 `GRAPH_LANG_TITLE = "https://gemea.ise.fiz-karlsruhe.de/graph/lang-title"` added. 467 468 ### 10.3 Whitespace-in-lang bug 469 470 `lang = "en en"` appears on record `LH3LUU63TUKZJHMQEILGTP3HZXZP5IFT` (`s2.sqlite`) on `ProvidedCHO.dcType` and `Concept[1].prefLabel`. Root cause: `Concept[1].about` contains two space-joined URIs (`"http://ddb.vocnet.org/hierarchietyp/ht007 http://ddb.vocnet.org/hierarchietyp/ht020"`); the DDB XML-to-JSON converter applied the same join to the `lang` attributes of the repeated element, concatenating `"en"` + `" "` + `"en"`. 471 472 Without the guard, `lang_coll.add("en en")` would produce `` ã an invalid N-Quads IRI. Fix in `value_to_nt_obj()`: 473 474 ```python 475 lang = val.get("lang") 476 if lang: 477 lang = str(lang).strip() 478 if lang and _invalid_bcp47(lang): 479 if lang_coll is not None and " " not in lang: 480 lang_coll.add(lang) 481 lang = "und" 482 ``` 483 484 `.strip()` handles leading/trailing whitespace. `" " not in lang` prevents any space-containing value from entering `lang_coll` (it still normalizes to `"und"`, so the literal output is correct). 485 486 ### 10.4 Test additions 487 488 Three new test classes in `test_transform.py`: 489 490 | Class | Tests | Covers | 491 |---|---|---| 492 | `TestIanaCollectionCodes` | 3 | Registry loaded (>0 codes); known collectives (`wen`, `gem`) present; valid individuals (`ger`, `eng`) absent | 493 | `TestInvalidBcp47` | 4 | Collective ã `True`; malformed ã `True`; valid codes ã `False`; `und` itself ã `False` | 494 | `TestValueToNtObjLangNorm` | 10 | `wen`/`gem`/`gerger` ã `@und`; valid lang unchanged; `lang_coll` populated for collective/malformed, empty for valid; `"en en"` ã `@und` and not added to `lang_coll`; leading/trailing whitespace stripped | 495 496 Total: 137 tests (135 passing; 2 pre-existing failures in `TestRetypeEntities` ã see ôÏ9.3). 497 498 --- 499 500 ## 12. Full-corpus run plan 501 502 The production orchestrator is `scripts/run-transform-sector.sh`. It splits the SQLite table into `--workers` chunks, launches one `python -m transform` OS process per chunk, waits for all to finish, then merges. 503 504 ```bash 505 # one sector, all defaults (50 workers, merge stats only): 506 bash scripts/run-transform-sector.sh --merge --sector s2 507 508 # merge including .nq: 509 bash scripts/run-transform-sector.sh --merge-all --sector s2 510 511 # custom worker count: 512 bash scripts/run-transform-sector.sh --merge --sector s2 --workers 20 513 ``` 514 515 Key defaults: `--sqlite-dir /data/gemea/sqlite`, `--scripts-dir /home/ann/goethe-faust/scripts`, `--output-dir /data/gemea/www/downloads/gemea/YYYYMMDD` (today's date). 516 517 **Merge output naming**: `merge.py` receives `--stem "$SECTOR"`, so all merged files are named `-stats.json`, `.log`, etc. (not the version-date directory name). Multiple sectors run sequentially into the same version dir produce non-colliding outputs. 518 519 **`--merge` vs `--merge-all`**: `--merge` passes `--skip-nq` to `merge.py` ã merges stats, werk_staging, errors, and logs only; `.nq` shards are retained for later concatenation. `--merge-all` also concatenates `.nq` shards into `.nq`. 520 521 Config files (`--alignment`, `--lido`, `--htype`, `--mediatype`, `--audio`) default to `output/config/` relative to the goethe-faust root derived from `--scripts-dir`. 522 523 Estimated wall time at 18.5M records: ~1ã1.5 h (7 parallel workers). 524
